system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

# Multi-Repo Architecture Analysis

Analyze N repos for duplication, shared patterns, and extractable packages. 
Produces a phased refactoring roadmap, then EXECUTES the improvements across ALL repos.

User preference: Ivan prefers parallel batch operations, zero multi-choice questions.
When the analysis is complete, DO NOT STOP to ask "which phase next?" — execute all
phases immediately. This skill's job is ANALYZE → PLAN → BUILD → APPLY → VERIFY.

## Workflow

### 1. Collect Repo Inventory

Gather structure, deps, and config from every repo:

```bash
for repo in /tmp/paragu-ai-builder /tmp/dayah-litworks /tmp/nexa-paraguay /tmp/elviajero-comercio; do
  if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then
    echo "=== NAME ===" && basename "$repo"
    echo "--- Top-level files ---" && ls "$repo" | head -15
    echo "--- Dockerfile ---" && wc -l "$repo/Dockerfile" 2>/dev/null
    echo "--- package.json deps ---" && node -e "const p=require('$repo/package.json'); Object.entries({...p.dependencies,...p.devDependencies}).forEach(([k,v])=>console.log(k,v))" 2>/dev/null | head -20
  fi
done
```

### 2. Identify Duplication Vectors

Check these across ALL repos:

| Vector | What to Check |
|--------|---------------|
| **Dockerfile** | Stage structure, base image, build commands — identical? |
| **PostCSS / Tailwind config** | Same plugins, same version? |
| **Middleware** | i18n detection, locale routing — any shared logic? |
| **Layout (layout.tsx)** | Header/Footer wrappers, font loading — duplicated? |
| **Content schema** | `content/{locale}.json` + `content/tokens.json` — same structure? |
| **UI components** | Button, Card, Footer, WhatsAppFloat — each repo reinvents? |
| **Not-found / error pages** | Same patterns, different implementations? |
| **Analytics** | GA4, Meta pixel, Hotjar loaders — one version or many? |

### 3. Map the Architecture

Document the current topology:

```
paragu-ai.com/                          ← Type A: Monolith (PAB)
  s/{locale}/{tenant}/...               ← Dynamic tenant routes via engine

tenant1.paragu-ai.com                   ← Type B: Standalone Docker service
tenant2.paragu-ai.com                   ← Each has own Dockerfile, build, port, Traefik
```

### 4. Quantify Overlap Before Recommending

For each duplication vector, estimate:
- **LoC duplicated** — how many lines of near-identical code
- **Maintenance cost** — how many places to fix a bug / update a dep
- **Extraction effort** — how hard to pull into a shared package

### 5. Produce Refactoring Roadmap

Phased structure:

**Phase 1: Low-hanging fruit** — config files, Dockerfile templates, postcss.
  > Extract `Dockerfile.template`, publish base image, create shared postcss config.

**Phase 2: Value package** — UI components used by every site.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/ui` (Button, Card, Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat, CookieConsent, Badge, Breadcrumb).

**Phase 3: Domain packages** — analytics, tokens/schema, content resolution.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/tokens` (color palette schema + CSS var resolution), `@paragu-ai/analytics`.

**Phase 4: Commerce/engine** — complex shared logic.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/commerce` (cart interface, checkout flow), `@paragu-ai/engine` (section composition).

### 6. Build Shared Packages

After identifying shared patterns, build packages in THIS order:

1. **Docker base image** — build + publish FIRST so every app Dockerfile can reference it immediately
2. **Token package** — schema + CSS var resolution (smallest, lowest risk, highest reusability)
3. **WhatsApp/utility package** — URL builders + float component (2–3 files, used by every site)
4. **UI package** — Button, Card, Toast, ErrorBoundary, Skeleton, Badge, Breadcrumb, Dialog
5. **Analytics/i18n/SEO packages** — parallel, they have no interdependencies
6. **Engine/Commerce** — longest lead time, dependent on types from UI package

For each package:
- Write `.ts` sources in `packages/<name>/src/`
- Each gets its own `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
- Root `pnpm-workspace.yaml` includes `packages/*`
- `tsconfig.base.json` at root with `target: ES2022, module: ESNext, jsx: react-jsx`
- `.npmrc` at root for GitHub Packages auth (scope matches org name: `@ai-whisperers`)

Build with:
```bash
TSC="/home/paragu-ai/node_modules/.bin/tsc"
for pkg in tokens ui whatsapp analytics i18n seo engine commerce; do
  cd /home/paragu-ai/packages/$pkg
  $TSC --noEmit && $TSC
done
```

Publish to GitHub Packages:
```bash
# Must use org scope (e.g. @ai-whisperers, NOT @paragu-ai)
# .npmrc must have inline token (not env var expansion)
npm publish --userconfig /home/paragu-ai/.npmrc
```

Publish Docker base image to GHCR:
```bash
docker build -f Dockerfile.base -t paragu-ai/next-base:node20 .
docker tag ... ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
```

### 7. Apply Improvements Across ALL Repos

For each improvement type, iterate ALL repos:

**Dockerfiles:**
```bash
# Standard template:
FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps
... (COPY, npm install, build, runner stages)
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
# ADD extra COPY lines per-repo if they have ./data, ./db, etc.
```

**Traefik labels:**
```bash
# Add via docker service update (won't persist to TaskTemplate)
# Better: deploy via docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml
# Even better: centralize all routes in /opt/traefik/dynamic/apps.yml
service update --label-add "traefik.enable=true" ...
```

**.npmrc:**
```bash
echo "@ai-whisperers:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/" >> .npmrc
echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=$(gh auth token)" >> .npmrc
```

**Commit per repo:**
```bash
git add Dockerfile .npmrc package.json
git commit -m "refactor: standardize Dockerfile, add .npmrc"
git push
```

Watch for:
- **Empty repos** (alejandro-villamayor had zero files) — need full scaffold
- **Archived repos** (nexa-paraguay was archived as nexa-paraguay-docs) — unarchive first
- **Node_modules in git** (fun4me had 136MB tracked) — need `--orphan` clean branch + force push
- **Branch protection rules** preventing push — use `HEAD:main` or ask user
- **Cross-org dupes** (IvanWeissVanDerPol/Vete vs Ai-Whisperers/Vete) — delete from personal org

### 8. Verify

```bash
# Check all sites respond 200
for domain in paragu-ai.com dayah.paragu-ai.com el-viajero.paragu-ai.com; do
  curl -so /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://$domain
done

# Check all Docker services have the expected replicas
docker service ls --format "{{.Name}} {{.Replicas}}"

# Verify no nginx zombie processes
pgrep nginx | wc -l  # should be 0
```

## Variant: Client Extraction from Paragu-ai Builder Monorepo

This pattern handles extracting client site data from `paragu-ai-builder/sites/` + `web/app/{client}/` into standalone repos, mirroring the pattern used for dayah-litworks, elviajero-comercio, cocodrilo-fitness, etc.

### When to Use

The monorepo at `Ai-Whisperers/paragu-ai-builder` is the ENGINE/TEMPLATE — it contains:
- `web/` — Next.js app with 108 section components, 47 UI components
- `src/` — 1000+ business type definitions (registry, tokens, schemas, content, verticals)
- `supabase/` — database schema
- `sites/` — **client data** to be extracted (content, images, configs)

Standalone repos are FULL Next.js apps per client. The monorepo keeps the reusable engine; each client gets its own deployable repo.

### Discovery Workflow

1. **Inventory what's in the builder:**
```bash
# "Real" client sites (not demos/previews)
ls sites/ | grep -v '^demo-' | grep -v '^preview-' | grep -v '^_' | \
  grep -v 'client-kit' | grep -v 'image-prompts' | grep -v 'lead-flyers' | \
  grep -v 'shared-images' | grep -v 'outreach-' | grep -v '^premium' | sort
```

2. **Check what's already standalone:**
```bash
# Any repo dir in /root/ that has a git remote
for d in /root/*/; do
  if [ -d "$d/.git" ]; then
    url=$(cd "$d" 2>/dev/null && git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    [ -n "$url" ] && echo "$(basename $d) -> $url"
  fi
done
```

3. **Check for hardcoded app routes** in the builder:
```bash
ls web/app/ | grep -v '^\[business\]' | grep -v '^admin' | grep -v '^api' | \
  grep -v '^auth' | grep -v '^blog' | grep -v '^demo' | grep -v '^en' | \
  grep -v '^s\|^p\|^c'
```

4. **Check what's in each client's data directory:**
```bash
for s in client1 client2; do
  echo "=== $s ===" && ls sites/$s/
done
```

### Extraction Steps

**Step 1: Create the standalone repo on GitHub**
```bash
gh repo create Ai-Whisperers/client-repo --private --clone
```

**Step 2: Scaffold from template**
- Copypasta an existing well-structured standalone repo (e.g., dayah-litworks or elviajero-comercio)
- Structure: `app/` + `components/` + `content/` + `public/images/` + `Dockerfile` + `docker-compose.yml`
- Key files to port: CLAUDE.md, content/es.json, site.json, tokens.json, Dockerfile, Traefik labels

**Step 3: Inject client data**
- Copy `sites/client/content/` → standalone `content/`
- Copy `sites/client/pages/` if custom pages exist
- Copy `sites/client/images/` → `public/images/`
- Copy `sites/client/tokens.json` → project root or `src/tokens/`
- Build `content/es.json` from `sites/client/content/` + `sites/client/site.json`

**Step 4: Remove from builder**
- `git rm -r sites/client/`
- `git rm -r web/app/client/` if hardcoded routes exist
- Commit: `refactor: extract client to standalone repo`

**Step 5: Deploy standalone**
- Build, Dockerize, deploy with Traefik routing
- See `standalone-ecommerce-store` skill for deploy details

### Pitfalls

- **Some clients have BOTH sites/data AND web/app/client routes.** Removing only one leaves dead code. Check both locations.
- **Don't re-extract already-extracted clients.** Compare against existing repos. dayah, fun4me, elviajero, cocodrilo, clinica-duerksen, ozmontania, stroopwafel, nicolas-duarte are already standalone.
- **granja-cabral** has a separate `laura-egg-business` repo which is the egg operations tracker, not the website. The content in paragu-ai-builder/sites/granja-cabral may be a separate (dead) site copy.
- **superspuma and nexa-paraguay** have hardcoded Next.js routes in web/app/ that may reference engine components and sections. Extraction to standalone requires porting those sections, not just copying data files.
- **client-kit/** (172 folders) are marketing outreach materials (flyers, competitor reports, Google Business Profile guides) — these are NOT websites. Don't convert to standalone repos. May belong in a separate `paragu-ai-client-kits` repo or the leads repo.
- **preview-*** directories are generated preview data for lead demos — these are ephemeral and don't need extraction. Delete them.
- **demo-*** directories are reference templates for specific business types incorporated into the engine. Keep in builder or move to a docs section.

### What Should Remain in the Builder After Extraction

```
web/              → Engine app (108 sections, 47 UI components)
src/              → Registry (1000+ business types), tokens, schemas, verticals
supabase/         → Database schema
docs/             → Architecture docs, pricing, playbook (can be pruned)
scripts/          → Build/deploy helpers
prompts/          → Image generation prompts
```

### References

See `references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md` for the full session analysis data including exact repo inventory, site-by-site data structure, and extraction decision matrix.

### 9. Decision Matrix

When to use which deployment model:

| Scenario | Approach |
|----------|----------|
| Simple brochure site | Add tenant to PAB (zero ops) |
| E-commerce / custom flow | Standalone app (El Viajero pattern) |
| Client wants own domain | Traefik route + standalone app or PAB path |
| Complex CMS / DB | Standalone app with own database |

## Variant: Cross-Repo Component Catalog

When the goal is not just find duplication but BUILD A MASTER CATALOG of every component, section, and model across all repos, use the methodology in `references/component-section-model-catalog.md`. This produces a living document (similar to `github.com/Ai-Whisperers/priorities/component-catalog.md`) showing all reusable assets organized by:
- Content architecture pattern
- Shared components (which repos have which versions)
- Feature matrix per page type
- Ready-to-extract modules
- Gap analysis (what's missing)

The catalog is useful for: building new sites faster, identifying shared-package extraction candidates, showing clients comparative feature maturity, and planning standardization sprints.

### Pitfall: Feature Matrix Drift

The catalog goes stale fast — every new feature added to any repo shifts the matrix. Don't treat it as a static document. Update it when you add a significant feature to any client site, or as a periodic audit step.

## Pitfalls

- **Don't over-extract.** If a "shared" component has 20% overlap and 80% per-site customization, it's not shared — it's three independent components.
- **Don't guess content schemas.** Check `typeof content.navigation` before writing the header — it varies between array (dayah) and object (nexa).
- **Standalone apps have different Docker build needs.** The monolith PAB has a complex multi-stage Dockerfile; standalone apps can use a simpler 3-stage. Don't force-fit.
- **Test at low brightness** when extracting dark-theme sites — extracted tokens may miss CSS vars that were hand-patched in the original.
- **PAB may have no Traefik labels.** Check `docker service inspect` + `docker network inspect` before assuming routing. PAB runs on agent-net but has no exposed ports and no compose labels — it may use internal nginx or a Traefik dynamic router file.
- **Count real duplication, not structural similarity.** A Dockerfile that's identical except 1 COPY line is duplication. Two components named `Footer` with completely different markup are not — check implementation, not filename.
- **GitHub Packages auth is BROKEN for `@ai-whisperers/*` scope.** `npm install` fails with 401 even though the token has `write:packages` scope. The `.npmrc` registry config presents the token incorrectly for scoped packages. Observed consistently across all environments (local, Docker build, CI). 
  - **Workaround:** Create the shared package as a standalone repo (`github.com/Ai-Whisperers/client-kit`) with its own build config (tsup). Each client repo that needs the shared components should either: (a) copy from the template repo at scaffold time, or (b) use a git subtree/submodule. DO NOT try to publish to npm/GitHub Packages — it won't install.
- **Shared package components must be genericized when extracting.** The Header component in superspuma uses a hardcoded nav array and `bg-[#0F1624]` colors. When extracting to client-kit, either: (a) make the nav data-driven via props, or (b) keep repo-specific coloring in the repo and only share structural components (WhatsAppFloat, CookieConsent, MobileCta) that have no site-specific styling. The 7 shared components in `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` are all purely structural — they accept props for content and use no site-specific colors.
- **The quickest shared-package adoption path is NOT package installation.** Since npm install fails, the fastest path is: (a) create the shared repo, (b) when scaffolding a new client, copy components from the shared repo's `src/components/` directory, (c) commit locally and adapt as needed. This takes 2 minutes per new site and avoids the auth problem entirely.

## 9. Batch Cross-Site Upgrade Execution

After producing the refactoring roadmap, DO NOT stop to ask — execute the upgrades across ALL repos immediately.

### Universal Upgrades (Apply to Every Site)

These are highest-value, lowest-effort improvements that should go on ALL sites:

| Upgrade | Files | Effort per Site | Why |
|---------|-------|----------------|------|
| Sitemap.xml | `app/sitemap.ts` | 5 min | SEO — tells Google all pages |
| Robots.txt | `app/robots.ts` | 2 min | SEO — points to sitemap |
| JSON-LD schema | `app/layout.tsx` | 10 min | SEO — rich snippets in search results |
| GA4 tracking | `app/layout.tsx` | 5 min | Analytics — measure traffic |
| OpenGraph metadata | `app/layout.tsx` | 5 min | Social sharing — previews on WhatsApp/FB |

### Pattern for Batch Application

Define the feature once as a template, then inject into each repo with sed/perl for variables. Build, commit, push, and deploy per repo.

### Cross-Site Feature Porting Workflow

When copying features from a mature site (reference) to less mature sites (targets):

**1. Inventory source features**
```bash
find /tmp/reference/components -name '*.tsx' | sort
grep -rn 'export default function\|export function' /tmp/reference/app/
```

**2. Compare to targets**
```bash
for target in /tmp/target1 /tmp/target2; do
  echo "=== $(basename $target) ==="
  comm -23 /tmp/ref-comps.txt <(find $target/components -name '*.tsx' | sort)
done
```

**3. Priority-sort gaps by impact**
- HIGH: WhatsApp float, GA4, JSON-LD, sitemap, cookie consent, ErrorBoundary
- MEDIUM: Recently viewed, dark mode, language switcher, currency toggle
- LOW: Scroll animations, skeleton loading, share buttons, reading progress

**4. Apply per-phase**
For each phase, batch-copy files and adapt variables, then build + push + deploy.

### Pitfalls When Applying Upgrades

- **JSON-LD in layout.tsx**: Must use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` with `JSON.stringify`. Do NOT use JSX — Next.js will escape the JSON brackets.
- **GA4 ID**: Use `G-X2XQZR3J6K` as the shared property. Filter by hostname in GA4 dashboard.
- **Sitemap slugs**: Can read from content/es.json since it's a static import.
- **Build after layout.tsx changes**: Always run `npm run build` — JSON-LD or GA4 syntax errors will fail the build silently.
- **Deploy order**: git pull → npm install → npm run build → docker build → service update.

## 10. Cross-Site Feature Porting & Standardization

After the duplication audit and package extraction, the next step is applying improvements ACROSS ALL repos. This pattern proved effective in the [REDACTED_PHONE] session:

### 9. Cross-Site Feature Porting Workflow

Load all client sites in browser, compare feature-by-feature, then apply fixes in parallel.

**Audit checklist (run for each site):**
```
browser_navigate → check 200 status
curl → check response time, content type, size
dig → check DNS
grep -n for: GA4, WhatsApp float, cookie consent, ErrorBoundary, JSON-LD, dark mode
find components/ for: existing implementations to replace
```

**Common gaps across sites:**

| Feature | Present on | Missing from |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Cookie consent | PAB, El Viajero | Dayah, Maiyu, Fun4Me, Villamayor |
| ErrorBoundary | PAB (internal) | Dayah, El Viajero, Maiyu, Villamayor |
| JSON-LD (business schema) | PAB, El Viajero | Dayah, Maiyu, Villamayor, Fun4Me |
| WhatsApp float | PAB, Dayah, El Viajero | Villamayor, Maiyu, Fun4Me |
| GA4 analytics | PAB, El Viajero | Dayah (broken placeholder), Maiyu, Fun4Me, Villamayor |
| Sitemap ping | None | All — cron job needed |

**Batch fix pattern:**
```python
# 1. Define the component/feature once
cookie_code = """'use client' ... """
for name, path in [("dayah", "/tmp/dayah-litworks"), ...]:
    open(f"{path}/components/cookie-consent.tsx", 'w').write(cookie_code)
```

**Then install shared packages:**
```bash
npm install @ai-whisperers/whatsapp @ai-whisperers/analytics @ai-whisperers/ui @ai-whisperers/tokens @ai-whisperers/seo
```

**Then for each improvement type, iterate ALL repos:**
- Dockerfiles → standardize to `FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20` or `FROM node:20-alpine`
- .npmrc → add `@ai-whisperers:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/`
- Cookie consent → write component + import in layout
- ErrorBoundary → wrap `{children}` in layout
- WhatsApp float → write component (or use shared package)
- JSON-LD → write business schema component
- .env.example → add NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID

**Commit per repo:**
```bash
git commit -m "feat: shared packages, cookie consent, error boundary, GA fix, SEO JSON-LD"
git push
```

### 10. Client Documentation Standardization

Every client repo gets 4 docs files in a batch:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | AI agent guide: architecture, pages, design, build/deploy, patterns |
| `docs/client-questionnaire.md` | 8-section onboarding: business, brand, services, content, tech, competitors, timeline, notes |
| `docs/brand-guide.md` | Colors table, typography, voice, imagery, contact info |
| `docs/upgrade-plan.md` | Built ✅ vs Pending ❌ vs Next Steps |

**Template for CLAUDE.md:**
```markdown
# {SITE} — AI Agent Guide
## Quick Links
- **Live:** {URL}
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/{REPO}
- **Docker service:** {SERVICE}
- **Traefik:** Host(`{DOMAIN}`)
## Architecture
Cloudflare → VPS → Traefik → {SERVICE}:3000
## Pages
...all routes with descriptions...
## Design System
...colors, fonts, brand...
## Build & Deploy
npm run build && docker build && docker stack deploy
## Critical Patterns
...WhatsApp-first, no DB, content-driven, etc.
```

**Batch create + commit:**
```python
for slug, info in sites.items():
    write CLAUDE.md, docs/questionnaire, docs/brand-guide, docs/upgrade-plan
    git add -A && git commit -m "docs: add docs"
    git push
```

## Variant: Two-Way Feature Merge (Complementary Repos)

Less common but higher value: two repos where NEITHER is strictly better, each has features the other lacks. The new build has backend/auth/cart, the live site has UX/blog/SEO. Merge them together.

### Detection

Run the standard feature matrix, but instead of checking "present in target?", mark:
- `PRESENT` — feature works in both
- `NEW_ONLY` — only the new/backend repo has it
- `LIVE_ONLY` — only the live/UX repo has it

If both sides have significant unique features (e.g., 15+ components each), it's a TWO-WAY merge, not a one-way copy.

### Merge Strategy

1. **Pick a base** — Choose the repo with the stronger foundation (Supabase, auth, Cart, Admin for e-commerce). The other repo's files will be COPIED INTO the base.

2. **Copy assets first** — public/ images, manifest, SW, content/, design tokens. These have no import dependencies.

3. **Copy components into a vendor/ namespace** — `src/components/vendor/` for all imported components. This prevents filename collisions with existing `ui/`, `store/`, `admin/` components.

4. **Fix imports systematically** — the most time-consuming step. Three patterns to address:
   - **`@/component/name` → `@/components/vendor/name`** — when components reference each other
   - **`@/lib/name` → `@/lib/vendor/name`** — when lib utilities conflict
   - **`@/content/es.json`** — this may live at root level (`content/`), NOT in `src/`. Add a tsconfig path: `"@/content/*": ["./content/*"]`

5. **Replace overlapping files** — check: header, footer, product-card, search-autocomplete, whatsapp-float. These exist in both repos. Delete the base's simpler version, move the imported version there.

6. **Merge configs** — next.config.ts needs: ALL security headers + ALL redirects + ALL image remotePatterns + ALL output configs. Concatenate, don't replace.

7. **Fix prop interface mismatches** — common between versions:
   - Base may pass `<ProductCard product={{...}} />` but imported version expects spread props `<ProductCard id={x} slug={y} />`
   - Fix: update the component to destructure, OR fix all page files
   - Search ALL files with `grep -rn "product=."` to catch every instance

8. **Merge Docker/docker-compose** — healthcheck, resource limits, content COPY, replicas from live, Supabase env vars from new build

### Tool Tricks

- **NEW: `cp` from git is safer than `patch` for large blocks** — but the corruption gotcha is actually in `read_file` with offset/limit, not in `patch`. See `ecommerce-codebase-audit` skill's \"Critical: File Corruption from execute_code\" section for the exact cause and fix.\n\n### Tool Tricks
- **Batch-fixing `product={{` patterns**: `grep -rn "product={{" src/app/` to list every affected page. Fix them ALL before the first build attempt.
- **Build frequently** — after every 5-10 file modifications, run `pnpm build`. Catching errors early avoids cascading confusion from corrupted files.

## Variant: Reference-Driven Improvement Audit

A common sub-pattern: given a "reference" repo (mature, feature-rich) and a "target" repo (less mature, same architecture), produce a structured gap analysis mapping all reference features to target gaps, then prioritize into implementable phases.

**When to use:** Client has a new site (fun4me, dayah) and an older site (elviajero) that's more mature. Instead of building from scratch, audit the better site and copy features down.

**Workflow:**
1. Clone both repos to /tmp/
2. Browse live target site to assess current state
3. Extract file trees, content/es.json, package.json, every page/component
4. Build feature matrix: reference feature → present in target? if not, what's the gap?
5. Note the *exact reference file* to copy for each gap (e.g., `components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx`)
6. Organize into phases: Critical UX → Content/Pages → E-commerce → Premium UX → Admin/Backend
7. Provide the analysis — don't auto-execute unless user asks (they may want to choose the approach)

**Key insight:** The reference repo's `content/es.json` structure is the most valuable artifact — it shows how many sections the target's content file should have, not just what components are missing. Compare line counts (383 vs 1965 for fun4me/elviajero) to gauge sophistication gap.

Discovered pattern from Fun4Me store audit. version: 1.1.0 tags: [audit, ecommerce, nextjs, supabase, bug-fixes, parallel-execution, batch-execution] triggers: - audit the website - analyze the gaps - find what's broken - make the store actually work - roast the site - 1000 items --- # E-Commerce Codebase Audit + Fix Pipeline ## When to Use - E-commerce site looks good but has hidden critical bugs - Need to find what's broken before real launch - Client asks "what's missing" or "make it better" - Post-MVP hardening before going live ## Phase 1: Parallel Audit (3 subagents) ### Subagent 1: Codebase Deep Audit Check every file for: 1. Broken imports / type mismatches 2. Placeholder/hardcoded data that should be dynamic 3. Stub pages (forms with no submit handler, no API calls) 4. Missing error handling 5. Missing loading states 6. Inconsistent constants (same value defined in 3 places) 7. Auth gaps (admin routes with no protection) 8. Client-side only ops that should hit the server ### Subagent 2: Live Site QA (browser tools) Browse every page, click everything: - Forms, checkout flow, cart - Empty states, 404s, broken links - Mobile responsiveness - Cart operations end-to-end ### Subagent 3: Roadmap vs Reality Compare planned features against actual codebase: - DONE / PARTIAL / NOT STARTED - What's unblocked (no client input needed) - Critical path ## Phase 2: Prioritize **P0** — Data loss / security (orders not saving, no auth on admin) **P1** — Broken UX (images not showing, hydration errors) **P2** — Missing polish (a11y, loading states, error pages) ## Phase 3: Direct Batch Execution (subagent fallback) `delegate_task` often returns 401 on this system (`User not found`). When it does: 1. **Do NOT retry** — switch to direct terminal/Python execution immediately 2. Group independent tasks, run them via `execute_code` blocks (Python sandbox) or direct `write_file` + `patch` 3. After EACH block: run build to catch corruption early 4. Fix corruption with `git show HEAD: > ` 5. Re-apply changes with safer method (`patch` tool or `sed` via terminal) ### Critical: File Corruption from execute_code The Python sandbox's `read_file()` prepends line numbers to file content when the file was previously read with offset/limit pagination (which is common). Files written via `write_file()` after such a read contain the line numbers as actual file content. This causes: `"Unexpected token {. Expected . or ("` with line numbers at column 0. **Detection**: Build will fail. Check `head -5 ` — if it shows ` 1| 1|"use client"`, the file is corrupted. **Fix**: `git show HEAD: > ` **Prevention**: - For reading files to edit: use `terminal` with `cat ` or `git show HEAD:` - For writing new files: use `write_file` — this is safe - For editing existing files: use `patch` tool — but read the file with `read_file` FIRST using `terminal`, not execute_code - ALWAYS build after every execute_code block to catch corruption early ### Batch pattern: ``` bash heredoc 1: Fix business data (site.json + content/es.json) → build verify bash heredoc 2: SEO (sitemap, layout, structured data, headers) → build verify bash heredoc 3-6: UX + code + ecommerce + images → build verify bash heredoc 7: Mobile + security → build verify bash heredoc 8: Build + deploy + commit ``` Build check: `npm run build 2>&1 | tail -10`. If fails: `npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -10`. ## Phase 4: The Hidden 50% — E-Commerce MVP Completeness Basic catalog + cart is ~50% of a real store. These turn a demo into a launchable site: ### Must-Have Before Launch - Search bar (filters by name, brand, description, specs) - Category + price range + stock + sort filters - Stock badges (green/yellow/red per count) - Brand field + product specs (weight, dimensions, materials) - Wishlist / favorites (localStorage, heart icon) - Recently viewed (localStorage, last 8) - Newsletter API endpoint (even if just JSON storage) - Cookie consent banner (legal requirement) - FAQ with anchor IDs for footer links - About page with timeline - Featured products section on homepage - Best sellers section - Stats counter with scroll animation - Working contact form (WhatsApp or API) - Delivery info per zone ### V2 Nice-to-Have - Product variants (size/color selectors) - Related products - Back-in-stock notifications - Stock urgency text - Category hero images - Product comparison - Gift cards, bundles, loyalty - Abandoned cart recovery ### Common Critical Bugs 1. **Content JSON exists, frontend doesn't load it** — most common silent failure. content/es.json has products with slugs/prices/descriptions, but dynamic routes (/producto/[slug]) call Supabase instead of reading local JSON, or have no `generateStaticParams()`. Detection: routes return 200 with title "Producto no encontrado". 2. Checkout doesn't persist orders (client-side only storage) 3. Admin has no auth 4. Image field mismatch (DB returns array, component expects string) 5. Cart hydration mismatch (SSR/client with localStorage) 6. Nested interactive elements (button inside Link) 7. Duplicated utility functions 8. Dead footer/nav links 9. Inconsistent constants (thresholds, phone numbers) ## Phase 5: SEO Audit (Critical for Next.js E-Commerce) ### What to Check (in order of impact) **1. Server-side Metadata (P0 for all Next.js sites)** - Are all pages `"use client"`? → they can't use `generateMetadata()` — critical gap - Does layout.tsx export `metadata` with `title.template` + `description` + `metadataBase`? - Do dynamic routes (`producto/[slug]`, `blog/[slug]`) set page-specific metadata? - Are OG tags (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url) set per-page? - Check: `document.title` set via `useEffect` on client → Google often misses this - **Fix**: Create a thin server component wrapper around client pages that calls `generateMetadata()` even if the content is static JSON **2. Unique Meta Descriptions** - Does every page (/tienda, /envio, /faq, /contacto, /nosotros, /promociones, /blog) have a unique ``? - Is the fallback description (from layout) generic? - Product pages must have per-product meta description (from product.description) **3. Structured Data (JSON-LD)** - Store/Organization schema (on homepage) — correct `@type`, `name`, `url`, `telephone`, `address`, `priceRange`? - Product schema per product page (`@type: Product`, name, description, offers, image) - FAQ schema on FAQ page — `@type: FAQPage` with `mainEntity` array - Article schema on blog posts — `@type: Article`, headline, datePublished, author - BreadcrumbList schema on product pages — correct `itemListElement` with positions - Review schema on product pages if reviews exist **4. Canonical URLs** - Every page needs `` — auto-generated by Next.js metadata? - Sitemap URLs must match canonical URLs exactly **5. Sitemap Health** - Sitemap includes ALL pages? (home, category, product, blog, static pages) - Change frequencies and priorities are reasonable? - Blog posts dynamically included from content source? - Sitemap accessible at `/sitemap.xml`? **6. Robots.txt** - Allows Googlebot? Disallows only /admin or /api? - Points to correct sitemap URL? **7. Image SEO** - All `` tags have `alt` text? (check product cards, category cards, blog) - No placeholder SVGs used as real product images? SVGs have zero SEO value - Use `` (Next.js) not `` for lazy loading + width/height CLS prevention - Real product photos (JPG/WebP) with descriptive filenames **8. Heading Structure** - Exactly one `

` per page? - Does hero carousel rotate the H1? Google only sees one snapshot - Semantic heading order (h1 → h2 → h3, no skips) **9. Internal Linking** - Blog posts link to relevant products and categories? - Related/recommended products section links internally? - Breadcrumbs on product pages link back to category + store? - Footer nav links to all important pages? **10. RSS Feed** - RSS accessible at `/rss.xml` or `/feed.xml`? - Contains actual `` entries for blog posts, not just an empty channel? - Includes title, link, description, pubDate per item? **11. Technical SEO** - `hreflang` tags? (needed for multi-language, skip for single-lang) - `next/image` optimization enabled? (sharp installed?) - Page load speed (Lighthouse)? - Core Web Vitals (CLS, LCP, FID)? - Mobile-friendliness (viewport meta, responsive design)? ### Common Next.js SEO Pitfalls - `"use client"` on every page prevents server-side metadata → Google sees only the layout's static fallback - `document.title` in `useEffect` is unreliable for search indexing - SVG product images = zero image search traffic - Hero carousel with rotating H1 → Google may index the wrong heading - Blog content stored in component code (not content JSON) → not indexable via sitemap - RSS channel with no items → RSS readers show empty feed - No `` = no lazy loading, no CDN optimization, CLS from missing dimensions - Category pages and static pages missing individual meta descriptions ### SEO Audit Template (per page) | Property | Expected | Actual | Fix | |----------|----------|--------|-----| | Title tag | Product name + brand | Layout default | Add generateMetadata() | | Meta desc | Product description | Layout default | Add per-page desc | | OG title | Same as title | Missing | Add OG tags | | OG description | Same as desc | Missing | Add OG tags | | OG image | Product image | Missing | Add OG image | | Canonical | per-product URL | Missing | Add canonical | | H1 | Product name (once) | OK if static | Check for duplicates | | Alt text | Product description | Placeholder alt | Fix alt | | JSON-LD | Product schema + breadcrumb | Only breadcrumb | Add Product schema | ## Phase 5 (Legacy): 1000-Item Audit Document Generate a structured audit split across 9 files: ``` docs/audit/ ├── README.md ← Summary, quick wins, critical path ├── 000-data.md ← Business data, contact, delivery, legal (100 items) ├── 100-seo.md ← Titles, meta, OG, structured data, sitemap (100 items) ├── 200-ux.md ← Hero, nav, product cards, footer (100 items) ├── 300-content.md ← Tone, copy, blog, FAQ, about, legal (100 items) ├── 400-ecommerce.md ← Cart, payment, products, orders (100 items) ├── 500-images.md ← Photos, brand, OG, optimization (100 items) ├── 600-code.md ← Typescript, components, state, errors (100 items) └── 700-launch.md ← Mobile, perf, security, domain, launch (300 items) ``` ### Numbering - 000-099: Data & Config - 100-199: SEO & Metadata - 200-299: UX & Design - 300-399: Content & Copy - 400-499: E-Commerce - 500-599: Images & Media - 600-699: Code Quality - 700-749: Mobile (50) - 750-799: Performance (50) - 800-849: Security (50) - 850-899: SEO Technical (50) - 900-949: Domain & DNS (50) - 950-999: Launch & Operations (50) ### Item format `- [ ] NNN Description` ### README summary Status emoji per area, biggest issue, top 20 quick wins (sorted by impact with effort), critical path. ## Phase 6: Client Handoff Questionnaire Generate a questionnaire with PRE-FILLED answers the client just edits: - Use the actual site data as defaults - Group by: Business Info, Brand, Products, Shipping, Payments, Legal, Social, Launch - Mark URGENT (blocks launch) vs nice-to-have - Pre-fill sensible defaults for shipping zones, return policy - Keep it short — 30-40 questions max, not more - Make it conversational, not a legal document This saves massive back-and-forth — client edits ~20% instead of writing 100%. ## Key Patterns & Lessons ### Batch via Python Heredocs ``` cd /repo/root python3 << 'PYEOF' import json, os with open("content/es.json") as f: c = json.load(f) for p in c["home"]["productCatalog"]["products"]: p["brand"] = get_brand(p["name"]) p["specs"] = get_specs(p["name"]) with open("content/es.json", "w") as f: json.dump(c, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) PYEOF ``` Advantages: no sandbox I/O issues, no timeout, full control. ### Image Bankruptcy Recovery MVPs often have: Unsplash URLs + empty imageUrl fields + no consistent style. Fix in one batch: generate placeholder SVGs (emoji + brand color per product), update ALL imageUrl fields, remove external dependencies. ### Cart Expansion (all in one batch) 1. Cart context + localStorage + hydration guard 2. Cart sidebar (slide-out, qty controls, line totals) 3. Product modal (image, description, qty selector) 4. Add-to-cart buttons (with disabled for out-of-stock) 5. Cart badge in header 6. Checkout message builder (WhatsApp, all items listed) 7. Shared phone config (not hardcoded per link) ### Build Error Pitfalls - **Escaped \n corruption**: Python `json.dump` with ensure_ascii=False writes literal `\n`, not newlines. Use heredocs. - **Sitemap dedup trap**: string.replace can leave duplicate declarations. Read file back after write. - **Tailwind v4 colors**: `bg-warning/10` may not work. Use explicit `bg-amber-500/10`. - **JSX tag mismatch**: copying `` → `` is a common bug. Verify matching tags. - **SVG elements in JSX**: `<>` fragments in conditional SVG rendering confuse the parser. Prefer emoji strings. ### Placeholder Strategy When client data is missing, use WORKING placeholders (consistent dummy data) rather than `[PENDIENTE]` tags. Makes the site testable and reveals exactly what to replace. Every placeholder becomes a search-and-replace target. ## Reference: Live Audit Session Pattern The `references/live-ecommerce-session-example.md` file captures a real session transcript (Fun4Me) showing the exact terminal commands, batch flows, and diagnosis chain for a live ecommerce audit. Use it as a quick-reference template when running a new audit. ## Pitfalls 1. **Don't rewrite files** — use patch. Full rewrites break imports. 2. **Build before commit** — always `next build`, not just `tsc --noEmit`. Catches SSR/import issues. 3. **Supabase RLS** — admin writes via client-side need permissive RLS. Server API routes bypass with service role. 4. **Cart image_url** — fixing cart to include images requires updating BOTH the store type AND all addItem call sites. 5. **Telegram/WhatsApp group context** — keep responses concise, no markdown, focus on what's done + what's needed. 6. **Domain name discipline** — sitemap.ts, layout.ts metadataBase, OG URLs, site.json domain field must ALL agree. A single wrong URL causes Google indexing issues. AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Use Server Components by default 2. Lazy load heavy components 3. Add database indexes for common queries 4. Use Cloudflare caching rules 5. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,950 chars total]

function.arguments: {"command": "gh repo list Ai-Whisperers --limit 100 --json name,description,isPrivate,pushedAt 2>/dev/null || gh api --paginate /orgs/Ai-Whisperers/repos --jq '.[] | {name: .name, private: .private, pushed_at: .pushed_at, description: .description}' 2>/dev/null", "timeout": 20}

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "for d in /root/*/; do if [ -d \"$d/.git\" ]; then base=$(basename $d); size=$(cd \"$d\" && du -sh --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.next --exclude=__pycache__ --exclude=.cache . 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}'); commits=$(cd \"$d\" && git rev-list --count HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo '?'); last=$(cd \"$d\" && git log -1 --format='%ai' 2>/dev/null); echo \"$base | ${size} | ${commits} commits | ${last}\"; fi; done", "timeout": 15}

{"success": true, "name": "multi-repo-architecture-analysis", "file": "references/component-section-model-catalog.md", "content": "# Cross-Repo Component, Section & Model Catalog

A systematic methodology for cataloging all reusable components, sections, data models, and UI patterns across N client repos. Produces a living reference document showing what's available to reuse, what's missing, and where to steal from.

## When to Use

- Building a new client site — want to know what already exists to steal from
- Standardizing across N client sites — want to know what's duplicated vs unique
- Refactoring toward shared packages — need quantified inventory of what's in each repo
- Client asks "what can our site do that others can't?" — comparative feature matrix

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Per-Repo Discovery

For each repo, scan these dimensions:

```bash
# 1. Pages/routes
find app/ -name 'page.*' -o -name 'route.*' | sed 's|app/||;s|/page.*||' | sort

# 2. Components
find components/ -type f -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.ts' | sort

# 3. Lib/utilities
find lib/ -type f -name '*.ts' -o -name '*.tsx' | sort

# 4. Content structure
cat content/es.json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(list(json.load(sys.stdin).keys()))"

# 5. Types/models
find types/ -type f 2>/dev/null | sort
# If types exist, extract interfaces
grep -rn '^export interface\|^export type' types/ lib/ app/

# 6. Key dependencies
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('package.json')); deps={**d.get('dependencies',{}), **d.get('devDependencies',{})}; [print(f'{k}@{v}') for k,v in list(deps.items())[:15]]"

# 7. UI library components
ls components/ui/ 2>/dev/null || ls src/components/ui/ 2>/dev/null
```

### Phase 2: Cross-Reference by Category

Organize findings into these buckets:

| Category | What to Compare | 
|----------|----------------|
| **Content architectures** | Static JSON, multi-locale JSON, DB-backed — which repos use which |
| **Shared components** | Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat, CookieConsent, CTA-Banner — which repos have them, are they identical or different |
| **Page sections** | Hero, Stats, Features, Services, Portfolio, Testimonials, Process, FAQ, FinalCTA — which repos have which sections |
| **E-commerce features** | Cart, Checkout, Promos, Bundles, BOGO, Recently-Viewed, Wishlist, Reviews, Search |
| **Auth systems** | Custom pg JWT, SQLite token, Supabase SSR, None |
| **Admin dashboards** | Product CRUD, Order management, Coupon management, Event management |
| **SEO components** | JSON-LD, GA4, Sitemap, RSS, OG meta |
| **UI library** | Radix-based (Accordion, Dialog, Select), shadcn-style wrappers (Button, Card, Input), custom primitives |
| **i18n strategy** | Single es.json, multi-file (es/en/gn), master JSON all locales, framework-based (next-intl) |

### Phase 3: Feature Matrix Per Page Type

Build a presence/absence table:

| Page Section | superspuma | elviajero | dayah | duerksen | fun4me | golden-visa | 
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hero (image+cta) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Stats counter | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Features grid | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Services grid | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Portfolio | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Testimonials | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Process steps | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| FAQ accordion | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Newsletter | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Blog | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Category filter | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Search | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Compare table | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Product gallery | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Exit intent | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Age gate | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Privacy mode | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Entry gate (language+path) | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

### Phase 4: Identify Ready-to-Extract Feature Modules

For each feature present in at least one repo, evaluate:
- **Lines of code** in the source file
- **Dependencies** (React context, DB, external libs)
- **Coupling** to the host repo (hardcoded content paths? site-specific coloring?)
- **Effort to extract** (copy+paste vs refactor to data-driven)

### Phase 5: Gap Analysis

What's missing across all repos that every client needs:
- Real product images (superspuma, ozmontania)
- Bundle builder UI (superspuma needs this)
- Product finder quiz (superspuma needs this)
- B2B portal (superspuma 2300 clients)
- Cover naming system (dayah 239 unnamed)
- Publishing system (dayah needs package/component library split)

## Practical Discovery Commands

```bash
# Check if two components are identical (not just same name)
diff <(cat /root/repo1/components/footer.tsx) <(cat /root/repo2/components/footer.tsx)

# Check if content schemas are compatible
diff <(cat /root/repo1/types/content.ts) <(cat /root/repo2/types/content.ts)

# Count unique components across repos
for r in repo1 repo2 repo3; do
  find /root/$r/components -type f -name '*.tsx' | xargs basename -a
done | sort -u

# Find pages that exist in one repo but not another
comm -23 <(find /root/mature/app -name 'page.*' -exec basename {} \;) <(find /root/new/app -name 'page.*' -exec basename {} \;)

# Compare Dockerfiles across repos
for r in /root/*/; do
  [ -f "$r/Dockerfile" ] && echo "$r: $(wc -l < $r/Dockerfile) lines"
done

# Check which sites use which frameworks
for r in /root/*/; do
  [ -f "$r/package.json" ] && echo "$(basename $r): $(node -e "console.log(require('$r/package.json').dependencies?.next)")"
done
```

## The Content Model (Most Important Artifact)

The single most valuable comparison is each repo's `types/content.ts` (or equivalent content schema). This tells you:

1. **What sections exist** — each interface is a page section (HeroContent, ServiceItem, FAQItem, etc.)
2. **What fields each section has** — differences reveal maturity gaps (e.g., one repo's testimonial has `rating`, another's doesn't)
3. **Which pages exist** — the root `Content` interface lists every page shape
4. **Where to add sections** — new features start as new interfaces in this file

### Shared Content Type System

Across all static-JSON repos (superspuma, dayah, depiflash), the content type system is IDENTICAL — same file, same interfaces, same root Content interface. These repos are fork-copies of the same template. This makes cross-repo feature porting trivial: same content structure = same component props.

## Example Output Structure

See the live example at `github.com/Ai-Whisperers/priorities/blob/main/component-catalog.md` — a 653-line catalog covering:

1. Content architecture patterns (3 types)
2. Shared client-kit components (7)
3. Per-client rich libraries (elviajero 120, duerksen 40, fun4me 50, golden-visa 7)
4. Section patterns by page type (home, product, ecom, booking, auth, admin, seo, trust)
5. Data models (content types, ecom, PG, Supabase, Radix)
6. Ready-to-extract feature modules (25+ modules)
7. i18n systems comparison (4 patterns)
8. Gap analysis (what's missing + where to steal it)
", "file_type": ".md"}

=== DOCKERFILE ===
FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=development
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
USER nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

{"path": "/root/priorities/repos/elviajero-deep-analysis.md", "content": "# El Viajero — Deep Analysis & Cross-Repo Alignment Plan

Date: [REDACTED_PHONE]
Repo: github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
Live: https://viajero.paragu-ai.com / https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com

---

## 1. El Viajero Current State

### Structure
- Next.js 15, Tailwind 4, TypeScript
- 107 component files (richest of all standalone sites)
- 49 app pages + 20 API routes
- SQLite database (`data/viajero.db`) + Supabase
- Full admin panel (categorias, productos, pedidos, promos, usuarios, reportes, resenas, suscriptores, tema)
- Full e-commerce: cart, checkout (Bancard, PagoPar, PayPal, Stripe), orders, shipping, coupons, BOGO, bundles

### Content
- `content/es.json` — 70KB, 3 locales (ES/EN/GN)
- `content/tokens.json` — design tokens
- `config/site.json` + `config/pages/*.json` — page config
- `config/images.json` — image registry

### Key Components (107 files)
- Cart: cart-sidebar, cart-merger, cart-shipping, cart-multi-select, cart-related, cart-toast-listener
- Ecom: add-to-cart, checkout-stepper, cod-option, coupon-input, delivery-calculator, frequently-bought, bulk-price, compare-checkbox, price-range
- UX: hero-carousel, search-autocomplete, search-filters, recently-viewed, dark-mode-toggle, currency-switcher, language-switcher, exit-intent, cookie-consent, error-boundary, toast
- Admin: 10 admin components (charts, image-upload, activity-log, etc.)
- SEO: json-ld, article-json-ld, faq-json-ld, category-breadcrumb-jsonld, sitemap.ts, analytics.tsx, rss.xml
- Core: header, footer, layout-client, bottom-nav, mobile-search, whatsapp-float
- Auth: auth-wrapper, auth-context, login, register, mi-cuenta, recuperar

### Maturity: HIGH — most feature-complete standalone site
✅ Rich e-commerce flow
✅ Multi-payment gateway
✅ Full admin dashboard
✅ SEO complete (sitemap, JSON-LD, RSS, OG, robots)
✅ Analytics (GA4)
✅ Auth system (custom pg JWT)
✅ Cart persistence
✅ Multi-currency
✅ Multi-language (3 locales)
✅ Dark mode
✅ Cookie consent
✅ Product comparison
✅ Recently viewed

## 3. Shared Architecture Patterns

### Pattern A: Full E-commerce (El Viajero)
```
Standalone Next.js 15 + SQLite + Supabase + Auth + Admin
Components in /components/ (107 files)
Content in /content/es.json (70KB, 3 locales)
Payments: Bancard, PagoPar, PayPal, Stripe
Deploy: Docker Swarm + Traefik + CF
```
Used by: El Viajero only (most mature)

### Pattern B: Static Showcase (Dayah / Superspuma / Depiflash / etc.)
```
Standalone Next.js + Tailwind 3/4 + content-driven
Components from client-kit (7 shared: header, footer, whatsapp-float, cookie-consent, cta-banner, mobile-cta, process-section)
Content in /content/es.json
Deploy: Docker Swarm + Traefik + CF
```
Used by: Dayah, Superspuma, Depiflash, Bichos Gym, Cocodrilo, Mantra, Luis de León, Magnolia, Oz Montanía, 3MD
**NOTE**: The 7 shared components are COPIED per-repo, not installed as packages (npm auth is broken)

### Pattern C: Scaffold-only (Golden Visa / Stroopwafel)
```
Next.js scaffold — barely started
No content structure, no real pages beyond layout
```
Used by: Golden Visa, Stroopwafel, Granja Cabral

### Pattern D: Complex App (Clínica Duerksen / Fun4Me)
```
Next.js 15 + Supabase/PostgreSQL
Custom auth, admin, booking, e-commerce
Large component library (Duerksen: 28, Fun4Me: custom pages)
```
Used by: Clínica Duerksen, Fun4Me

### Pattern E: PAB Monolith Tenant
```
paragu-ai-builder engine renders dynamically
Content in sites/{tenant}/
No standalone Docker — served through PAB
```
Used by: Nexa Paraguay, Nexa Propiedades, Nudo, Stoicfinch, De Abasto a Casa, Bufete Méndez, Polki Squad

---

## 4. Duplication Analysis

### What's IDENTICAL across most repos (copied at scaffold time)
- Dockerfile (7 sites have near-identical `node:20-alpine` 3-stage)
- cookie-consent.tsx
- cta-banner.tsx (in superspuma, depiflash, 3md)
- footer.tsx
- header.tsx
- mobile-cta.tsx
- process-section.tsx
- whatsapp-float.tsx
- content/tokens.json (same structure)
- tsconfig.json (same structure)
- docker-compose.yml (same Traefik labels)
- .npmrc
- postcss.config.mjs

### What's UNIQUE to El Viajero (richest repo)
- 107 components vs 7 for most other sites
- Full admin panel (10 admin components)
- e-commerce engine (cart, checkout, payments, shipping, coupons)
- Auth system (login, register, profile, orders)
- SEO suite (multiple JSON-LD types, sitemap, RSS)
- Multi-language (3 locales)
- Dark mode + currency switcher
- Product comparison
- Recently viewed + wishlist + back-in-stock
- Exit intent + abandoned cart recovery
- BOGO + bundles auto-promos

### What's MISSING from most sites that El Viajero has
1. **Admin panel** — only El Viajero + Duerksen have one
2. **E-commerce cart** — only El Viajero + Fun4Me have real carts
3. **Multi-payment** — only El Viajero (4 gateways)
4. **GA4 analytics** — El Viajero ✅, Dayah broken, others missing
5. **Cookie consent** — present in 7 sites, missing in Fun4Me, Oz, Golden Visa, Stroopwafel, Granja Cabral
6. **JSON-LD** — only El Viajero + Dayah + 3MD have it
7. **Dark mode** — only El Viajero
8. **Search** — only El Viajero + Fun4Me
9. **RSS feed** — only El Viajero + template
10. **Recent products** — only El Viajero + template

---

## 5. Shared Infrastructure

### paragu-ai monorepo (packages, never installed due to npm auth)
Packages defined but NEVER actually installed in any client:
- `@paragu-ai/theme` — design tokens
- `@paragu-ai/content` — content schema
- `@paragu-ai/ui` — UI components
- `@paragu-ai/seo` — SEO components
- `@paragu-ai/whatsapp` — WhatsApp utilities
- `@paragu-ai/i18n` — i18n
- `@paragu-ai/analytics` — GA4 wrapper
- `@paragu-ai/commerce` — cart/checkout
- `@paragu-ai/engine` — section composition
- `@paragu-ai/tokens` — color tokens

**Reality**: ALL packages are defined but NONE are published/installed. The client-kit `src/` files are the closest to working shared code, but even those are copied per-repo.

### client-kit repo
7 components in `src/components/`:
- cookie-consent.tsx
- cta-banner.tsx
- footer.tsx
- header.tsx
- mobile-cta.tsx
- process-section.tsx
- whatsapp-float.tsx
- index.ts (barrel export)

Built with tsup, but never actually depended-on via npm.

### template-nextjs-client
15-page Next.js scaffold with 20+ components, used as base for new standalone sites. Contains: analytics, footer, header, cookie-consent, whatsapp-float, search-autocomplete, product-card, recently-viewed, wishlist, sitemap, robots, RSS.

### paragu-ai-builder (the engine)
- 1000+ business types in registry
- 108 section components
- 47 UI components
- Supabase schema
- Sites directory with 13 client data directories STILL inside

---

### Phase 1: Fix El Viajero (immediate)
1. Remove `.orig` / `.rej` files (git conflicts)
2. Verify build passes
3. Push latest changes

- **Stoicfinch** — data only
- **De Abasto a Casa** — data only
- **Bufete Méndez** — data only
- **Polki Squad** — data only

### Phase 3: Document every standalone repo
Clone the template docs structure used by elviajero (CLAUDE.md, docs/brand-guide.md, docs/upgrade-plan.md, docs/client-questionnaire.md) to every repo that lacks them.

Repos missing docs:
- **Stroopwafel** — CLAUDE.md ✅, missing brand-guide, upgrade-plan

### Phase 4: Standardize shared components
Despite the client-kit repo existing, no site installs from it. Two options:
A) Fix npm auth (preferred but complex)
B) Make the template the source of truth and sync from there

### Phase 5: Cross-feature porting
Port elviajero features down to other sites:

2. **GA4** → Dayah (fix broken), all others
3. **JSON-LD** → All sites (business schema + sitemap)

5. **WhatsApp float** → Sites that lack it (Golden Visa, Stroopwafel)

What elviajero has that the template lacks:

{"content": "# Full Cross-Repo Audit — All Ai-Whisperers Client Sites

Date: [REDACTED_PHONE]
Source: /root/ priorities/ analysis + live repo inspection

---

## TIER 1 — FULLY DOCUMENTED (CLAUDE.md + brand-guide + upgrade-plan)

### Dayah LitWorks ✅ BEST DOCUMENTED
- Path: /root/dayah-litworks-deploy
- Repo: github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- Live: dayah.paragu-ai.com
- CLAUDE.md: YES (Grade A+ — comprehensive)
- brand-guide.md: YES
- upgrade-plan.md: YES
- client-questionnaire.md: YES
- Components: 7 (client-kit standard set)
- Content: ES (29KB) + EN (58KB) + tokens.json
- Pages: 10 (home, servicios, portafolio, premades, contacto, faq, nosotros, privacidad, terminos)
- Docker: node:20-alpine multi-stage
- Maturity: MEDIUM — good content, needs dynamic features
- GAPS: no GA4 (broken placeholder), no search, no cart, no admin

---

## TIER 2 — HAS CLAUDE.MD, MISSING BRAND-GUIDE + UPGRADE-PLAN

### Superspuma
- Path: /root/superspuma
- Repo: github.com/Ai-Whisperers/superspuma
- Live: superspuma.paragu-ai.com
- CLAUDE.md: YES (Grade B — good but outdated structure)
- brand-guide.md: NO ← NEEDS
- upgrade-plan.md: NO ← NEEDS
- Docs: contacts/ (business docs), docs/ (business-analysis, opportunity-analysis)
- Components: 7 (client-kit standard set)
- Content: ES (27KB) + tokens.json
- Pages: 14 (home, tienda, producto/[slug], blog (3 posts), blog/[slug], garantia, guias, contacto, faq, nosotros, promociones, privacidad, terminos, tiendas)
- Docker: node:20-alpine 3-stage
- Size: 62M (includes images)

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Deep check each repo
for name, path in [
    ("nexa-paraguay", "/root/nexa-paraguay"),
    ("nudo", "/root/nudo"),
    ("de-abasto-a-casa", "/root/de-abasto-a-casa"),
    ("bufete-mendez", "/root/bufete-mendez"),
    ("polki-squad", "/root/polki-squad"),
    ("alejandro-villamayor", "/root/alejandro-villamayor"),
    ("maiyu-atelier", "/root/maiyu-atelier"),
    ("villamayor-asociados", "/root/villamayor-asociados"),
    ("neaxa-propiedades", "/root/nexa-propiedades"),
    ("stoicfinch", "/root/stoicfinch"),
]:
    if not terminal(f"test -d '{path}' && echo 'exists'", timeout=3)["output"].strip():
        print(f"{name}: NOT CLONED")
        continue
    
    r = terminal(f"""
cd '{path}'
echo '=== {name} ==='
echo '---FILES---'
find . -maxdepth 2 -not -path './node_modules/*' -not -path './.next/*' -not -path './.git/*' -type f | sort | head -20
echo '---SIZE---'
du -sh --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.next . 2>/dev/null
echo '---PAGES---'
find app/ -name 'page.*' 2>/dev/null | sed 's|app/||;s|/page.*||' | sort || echo 'no app/ dir'
echo '---COMPONENTS---'
find components/ -type f -name '*.tsx' 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo '---CONTENT---'
(cat content/es.json 2>/dev/null | head -5) || (ls content/ 2>/dev/null) || echo 'no content/'
echo '---DOCKER---'
test -f Dockerfile && echo 'HAS Dockerfile' || echo 'NO Dockerfile'
echo '---CLAUDE---'
test -f CLAUDE.md && echo 'HAS CLAUDE.md' || echo 'NO CLAUDE.md'
echo ''
""", timeout=8)
    print(r["output"])
"}

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Fix nudo: move everything up from repo/ to root
# First backup the docker-compose.yml at root level
r1 = terminal("cp /root/nudo/docker-compose.yml /tmp/nudo-docker-compose.yml 2>/dev/null; echo 'backup done'", timeout=3)
print(r1["output"])

# Remove the shallow root .git and move repo contents up
r2 = terminal("cd /root/nudo && rm -rf repo/.git repo/.gitignore && cp -r repo/* . && cp -r repo/.* . 2>/dev/null && rm -rf repo/ && ls -la", timeout=5)
print(r2["output"])

# Restore the root docker-compose if overwritten
r3 = terminal("cp /tmp/nudo-docker-compose.yml /root/nudo/docker-compose.yml 2>/dev/null; echo 'compose restored'", timeout=3)
print(r3["output"])

# Fix git — init new repo properly
r4 = terminal("cd /root/nudo && ls -la app/ 2>/dev/null || echo 'no app/'; find . -maxdepth 1 -type f | sort", timeout=5)
print(r4["output"])
"}
