function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import write_file

# Batch 1: Write CLAUDE.md for repos that don't have one
# 1a. Nexa Paraguay
claude_nexa = """# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://nexaparaguay.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay
- **Docker service:** nexa_web (2 replicas)
- **Traefik:** Host(`nexaparaguay.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → nexa_web:3000

## Pages
- Home — relocation landing with stats, features, testimonials
- Blog with categories and posts
- Contact form → WhatsApp
- FAQ (10+ items on relocation process)
- About / Nosotros
- Services (visas, residency, property, business setup)
- Privacy policy, Terms

## Design System
- Professional, trustworthy blue/navy palette
- Font: Inter / system sans-serif
- Voice: Informative, warm, bilingual (ES/EN)

## Content
4 locales: content/es.json, en.json, de.json, nl.json
All text, services, FAQ, testimonials editable in content files.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml nexa
```

## Critical Patterns
- Multi-language (ES/EN/DE/NL) — most international client
- WhatsApp-first contact (all CTAs link to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Lead capture via contact form → email notification
- Document-heavy content (visa guides, property listings)
"""

write_file("/root/nexa-paraguay/CLAUDE.md", claude_nexa)

# 1b. Nudo
claude_nudo = """# Nüdo — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://nudo.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nudo
- **Docker service:** nudo_web (2 replicas)
- **Traefik:** Host(`nudo.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → nudo_web:3000

## Pages
- Home — hero + about + music + merch + events + lyrics + contact
- Single-page app with sections

## Sections (components/sections/)
- hero-section — band intro with logo/graphic
- about-section — band biography
- music-section — embedded tracks / discography
- lyrics-section — song lyrics display
- merch-section — merchandise catalog
- events-section — upcoming shows / past shows
- contact-section — booking inquiries → WhatsApp
- header-section — navigation
- footer-section — links + social media

## Design System
- Dark theme — hardcore metal aesthetic
- High-contrast typography
- Voice: Raw, intense, Spanish

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t nudo:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml nudo
```

## Critical Patterns
- Single-page app with sections (scroll navigation)
- No e-commerce (merch links to external)
- Music embedded via external players
- WhatsApp contact for booking

repos = [
    ("/root/superspuma", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan, CLAUDE.md update"),
    ("/root/fun4me-store", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/depiflash", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),

    ("/root/stroopwafel-huis", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/bichos-gym", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/cocodrilo-fitness", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/mantra-spa", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/luis-de-leon-concept", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/magnolia-peluqueria", "docs: add brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/3md-website", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/ozmontania-website", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/clinica-duerksen", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/granja-cabral", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/nudo", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan; fix repo structure"),
    ("/root/nexa-paraguay", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/de-abasto-a-casa", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/bufete-mendez", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/polki-squad", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
    ("/root/alejandro-villamayor", "docs: add CLAUDE.md, brand-guide and upgrade-plan"),
]

push_results = []
for path, msg in repos:
    r = terminal(f"cd '{path}' && git add -A && git commit -m '{msg}' && git push origin HEAD 2>&1 | tail -3", timeout=15)
    commit_result = r["output"].strip() or "committed"
    branch = path.split("/")[-1]
    push_results.append(f"{branch}: {commit_result}")

for pr in push_results:
    print(pr)
"}

# Superspuma — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://superspuma.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/superspuma
- **Docker service:** superspuma_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`superspuma.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → superspuma_web:3000

## Pages
Home, Tienda, Producto, Nosotros, Tiendas, Guías, Garantía, FAQ, Contacto, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Light theme (navy/white). Primary: #0F1624. Secondary: #3A4A5D. Accent: #B3C7E3. Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Open Sans (body). Voice: professional, informative, Spanish.

## Content
All content in `content/es.json`. Products, FAQ, testimonials, stores — all there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t superspuma:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml superspuma
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Light theme with navy accents
- 24 products with real PYG pricing

```

## Critical Patterns

- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary booking channel
- Coral/teal gradient theme
- Mobile laser service — no fixed location

[Subdirectory context discovered: clinica-duerksen/AGENTS.md]
# AGENTS.md - Development Guidelines for Clínica Duerksen

## Project Overview
Dental clinic website for Dr. Victor Duerksen in Asunción, Paraguay.
Built with Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, and Supabase.

## Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (App Router)
- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **Email**: Resend
- **Animations**: CSS (reduced-motion aware)
- **Deployment**: Docker + Traefik

## Project Structure
```
app/           → Next.js App Router pages and layouts
components/    → React components
  layout/      → Header, Footer, Navigation
  home/        → Homepage sections
  ui/          → shadcn/ui primitives
  seo/         → SEO components (JsonLd, etc.)
lib/           → Utilities, helpers, configs
  supabase.ts  → Supabase client helpers
  constants.ts → Business info, brand constants
  utils.ts     → Utility functions (cn, etc.)
public/        → Static assets
  images/      → Optimized images
  icons/       → Favicon, app icons
```

## Brand Identity
- **Primary**: #1B4D7A (deep medical blue) - trust, professionalism
- **Secondary**: #4A9BD9 (friendly blue) - approachability
- **Accent**: #D4A843 (warm gold) - quality, premium
- **Background**: #FFFFFF / #F8FAFB
- **Text**: #1A1A2E
- **Font**: Inter (all weights)

## Coding Standards

### TypeScript
- Use strict TypeScript - no `any` types
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Export types from the same file as their usage
- Use `as const` for constant objects

### Components
- Use functional components with arrow functions
- Co-locate component-specific types in the same file
- Use named exports (not default) for components except pages
- Keep components focused - extract logic to custom hooks

### Styling
- Use Tailwind CSS utility classes
- Use `cn()` helper for conditional classes
- Use CSS variables via shadcn/ui theme for brand colors
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Prefer `brand-primary`, `brand-secondary`, `brand-accent` for direct brand color access

### File Naming
- Components: PascalCase (e.g., `HeroSection.tsx`)
- Utilities: camelCase (e.g., `formatPhone.ts`)
- Constants: camelCase file, UPPER_CASE exports

### SEO
- Every page must have proper metadata
- Use semantic HTML (header, main, nav, section, article, footer)
- Include structured data (JSON-LD) for local business
- Optimize images with next/image
- All content in Spanish (es) by default

### Performance
- Use Next.js Image component for all images
- Lazy load below-the-fold content
- Use dynamic imports for heavy components
- Keep bundle size minimal
- Target Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1

### Git
- Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, style:, refactor:
- Branch from main
- Keep commits atomic and well-described
- Run `npm run build` before pushing

### Testing (future)
- Component tests with React Testing Library
- E2E tests with Playwright
- Test accessibility with axe-core

# AGENTS.md - Development Guidelines for Clínica Duerksen

## Project Overview
Dental clinic website for Dr. Victor Duerksen in Asunción, Paraguay.
Built with Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, and Supabase.

## Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (App Router)
- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **Email**: Resend
- **Animations**: CSS (reduced-motion aware)
- **Deployment**: Docker + Traefik

## Project Structure
```
app/           → Next.js App Router pages and layouts
components/    → React components
  layout/      → Header, Footer, Navigation
  home/        → Homepage sections
  ui/          → shadcn/ui primitives
  seo/         → SEO components (JsonLd, etc.)
lib/           → Utilities, helpers, configs
  supabase.ts  → Supabase client helpers
  constants.ts → Business info, brand constants
  utils.ts     → Utility functions (cn, etc.)
public/        → Static assets
  images/      → Optimized images
  icons/       → Favicon, app icons
```

## Brand Identity
- **Primary**: #1B4D7A (deep medical blue) - trust, professionalism
- **Secondary**: #4A9BD9 (friendly blue) - approachability
- **Accent**: #D4A843 (warm gold) - quality, premium
- **Background**: #FFFFFF / #F8FAFB
- **Text**: #1A1A2E
- **Font**: Inter (all weights)

## Coding Standards

### TypeScript
- Use strict TypeScript - no `any` types
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Export types from the same file as their usage
- Use `as const` for constant objects

### Components
- Use functional components with arrow functions
- Co-locate component-specific types in the same file
- Use named exports (not default) for components except pages
- Keep components focused - extract logic to custom hooks

### Styling
- Use Tailwind CSS utility classes
- Use `cn()` helper for conditional classes
- Use CSS variables via shadcn/ui theme for brand colors
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Prefer `brand-primary`, `brand-secondary`, `brand-accent` for direct brand color access

### File Naming
- Components: PascalCase (e.g., `HeroSection.tsx`)
- Utilities: camelCase (e.g., `formatPhone.ts`)
- Constants: camelCase file, UPPER_CASE exports

### SEO
- Every page must have proper metadata
- Use semantic HTML (header, main, nav, section, article, footer)
- Include structured data (JSON-LD) for local business
- Optimize images with next/image
- All content in Spanish (es) by default

### Performance
- Use Next.js Image component for all images
- Lazy load below-the-fold content
- Use dynamic imports for heavy components
- Keep bundle size minimal
- Target Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1

### Git
- Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, style:, refactor:
- Branch from main
- Keep commits atomic and well-described
- Run `npm run build` before pushing

### Testing (future)
- Component tests with React Testing Library
- E2E tests with Playwright
- Test accessibility with axe-core

## CRITICAL ISSUES

# Clear old .next output
rm -rf .next/standalone
npm run build
```

### P0 — Dirty Artifacts (3 files)

Old docker image vs new code — needs rebuild + redeploy.

- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio

- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter (all). Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) - shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content still lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) now comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- SQLite database for orders and admin
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

Node 20 + npm 10. The real issue is that the Dockerfile COPY path is wrong. The standalone dir contains `elviajero-comercio/` (the package name). The Dockerfile copies from `standalone/elviajero-comercio` to WORKDIR. Let me check if the root server.js is inside that dir.

The structure inside Docker builder should be the same. The issue might be that `npm run build` inside Docker doesn't output to `.next/standalone/elviajero-comercio/` because of something different in the Docker environment. Let me try a simpler Dockerfile that directly copies the standalone output.

Actually, the fix: reference the correct path. The COPY should be:

```dockerfile
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
```

This copies the ENTIRE standalone directory (including `elviajero-comercio/` subdir and `package.json` at root) and then we `WORKDIR elviajero-comercio/`. Or simpler:

```dockerfile
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone/ ./
```

This copies everything from standalone to WORKDIR. But the `package.json` at standalone root and `elviajero-comercio/` subdir both get copied.

Wait — the issue might be the npm install. The builder stage does `npm run build` but `node_modules` from deps stage are copied. Inside Docker with `node:20-alpine`, `npm install` might interpret `package-lock.json` differently.

Let me try a different approach — simplify the Dockerfile to work reliably.

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm ci --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/data ./data
CMD node server.js
", "path": "/root/elviajero-comercio/Dockerfile"}

{"output": "Background process started", "session_id": "proc_4c9789330fad", "pid": 2974417, "exit_code": 0, "error": null} [Subdirectory context discovered: /app/AGENTS.md] # Paragu-AI Builder - AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Use Server Components by default 2. Lazy load heavy components 3. Add database indexes for common queries 4. Use Cloudflare caching rules 5. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,940 chars total]

FAQ completo con 10 accordeones. Sin errores en consola. El sitio funciona impecable.

**Resumen final:**

**Phase 1 ✅**
- 3 dirty files eliminados
- metadataBase corregido
- Dockerfile reconstruido
- Docker build + deploy exitoso
- Pusheado a GitHub

**Phase 2 ✅**
- 16/16 páginas → 200 OK
- Sin errores de JavaScript en consola
- Catálogo: 34 productos, 30 marcas, 7 categorías
- Filtros: categoría, marca, precio, disponibilidad, orden
- Búsqueda, carrito, favoritos, cambio de moneda USD/PYG, modo oscuro
- FAQ con 10 accordeones, footer completo con redes
- WhatsApp float, cookie consent
- SEO: JSON-LD store schema, OpenGraph, sitemap, RSS

**Pendiente para otro día:**
- Tests de checkout completo (requiere login + datos de pago reales)
- Tests de admin panel (requiere credenciales)
- Tests de pasarelas de pago (Bancard, PagoPar, PayPal, Stripe)
- Finalizar migración Supabase (37 archivos en staging)
continue testubg work look for abstractions factories and things to refactor

path = "/root/elviajero-comercio"

print("=== 1. INTERFACE / TYPE EXPORTS ===")
r = terminal(f"cd {path} && grep -rn '^export interface\\|^export type\\|^export abstract' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' lib/ components/ app/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | head -40", timeout=8)
print(r["output"])

print("\n=== 2. FACTORY PATTERNS ===")
r2 = terminal(f"cd {path} && grep -rn 'factory\\|Factory\\|create[A-Z]\\|build[A-Z]\\|make[A-Z]\\|generate[A-Z]' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' lib/ components/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | head -20", timeout=8)

print(r3["output"])
"}

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

print("=== 4. DUPLICATED UTILITY FUNCTIONS ===")
r = terminal("cd /root/elviajero-comercio && grep -rn 'function \\|=> {' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' lib/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'node_modules\\|import\\|interface\\|type\\|export const' | head -40", timeout=8)
print(r["output"])

print("\n=== 5. HARDCODED STRINGS / PHONE / WHATSAPP ===")
r2 = terminal("cd /root/elviajero-comercio && grep -rnE '\"[0-9]{10,}\"|wa\\.me|whatsapp|phone|tel:' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' lib/ components/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | head -15", timeout=8)
print(r2["output"])

print("\n=== 6. LOCALSTORAGE KEYS (refactoring target) ===")
r3 = terminal("cd /root/elviajero-comercio && grep -rn 'localStorage\\.' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' lib/ components/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | head -20", timeout=8)
print(r3["output"])
"}

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

print("=== 7. COMPONENT ABSTRACTION OPPORTUNITIES ===")
r = terminal("cd /root/elviajero-comercio && for f in components/*.tsx; do lines=$(wc -l < \"$f\"); [ \"$lines\" -gt 200 ] && echo \"LARGE: $f ($lines lines)\"; done", timeout=5)
print(r["output"])

print("\n=== 8. SAME PATTERN IN MULTIPLE FILES ===")
r2 = terminal("cd /root/elviajero-comercio && grep -rn 'useEffect\\|useState' --include='*.tsx' components/ 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10", timeout=8)
print(r2["output"])

print("\n=== 9. API ROUTE PATTERNS ===")
r3 = terminal("cd /root/elviajero-comercio && for f in app/api/*/route.ts app/api/**/*/route.ts; do [ -f \"$f\" ] && echo \"$(wc -l < $f) $f\"; done 2>/dev/null | sort -rn | head -15", timeout=8)
print(r3["output"])
"}

"use client"
import { useState, useEffect } from "react"
import Link from "next/link"
import { usePathname, useRouter } from "next/navigation"
import { createClient } from "@/lib/supabase/client"
import type { User as SupabaseUser } from "@supabase/supabase-js"

interface Profile {
  id: string
  name: string
  email: string
  role: string
}

export function useAdminAuth() {
  const [authed, setAuthed] = useState(false)
  const [admin, setAdmin] = useState<Profile | null>(null)
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
  const supabase = createClient()
  const router = useRouter()

  useEffect(() => {
    supabase.auth.getSession().then(async ({ data: { session } }) => {
      if (!session) { setLoading(false); return }
      const { data } = await supabase.from("profiles").select("*").eq("id", session.user.id).single()
      if (data && data.role === "admin") {
        setAuthed(true)
        setAdmin({ id: data.id, name: data.name, email: session.user.email || "", role: data.role })
      }
      setLoading(false)

{"code": "from hermes_tools import write_file

analysis = """# El Viajero — Refactoring Targets & Abstractions

Date: [REDACTED_PHONE]
Repo: /root/elviajero-comercio

---

## P0 — HIGH IMPACT REFACTORS

### 1. Payment Gateway Factory
**Files:** `app/api/checkout/bancard/route.ts` (33L) + `pagopar/route.ts` (34L) + `paypal/route.ts` (43L) + `stripe/route.ts` (40L)
**Pattern:** 4 routes with ~90% identical boilerplate (validate cart, create order, return URL)
**Proposal:** `lib/payment/factory.ts` — one generic handler that receives gateway name, delegates to gateway-specific adapter
```typescript
// Instead of 4 route files:
POST → /api/checkout/[gateway]  // bancard | pagopar | paypal | stripe
```
**Effort:** 2h
**Benefit:** Adding new gateway = write adapter only (20 lines)

### 2. DB CRUD Factory
**Files:** `app/api/db/orders/route.ts` (37L) + `products/route.ts` (27L) + `promos/route.ts` (38L) + `reviews/route.ts` (33L) + `users/route.ts` (32L)
**Pattern:** 5 routes, each has GET (list) + POST (create) with almost identical Supabase queries
**Proposal:** `lib/db/crud-factory.ts`
```typescript
createCrudRoutes({ table: "products", adminOnly: true })
// Returns { GET, POST } handlers
```
**Effort:** 1h
**Benefit:** CRUD for new entities = 1 line

### 3. Supabase Client Consolidation
**Files:** `lib/supabase/client.ts` + `browser.ts` + `server.ts` + `middleware.ts` + `admin.ts` = 5 files
**Issue:** 5 different ways to create Supabase clients, some redundant (`client.ts` re-exports `browser.ts`)
**Proposal:** Consolidate to 3:
- `lib/supabase/browser.ts` — browser client
- `lib/supabase/server.ts` — server component client  
- `lib/supabase/admin.ts` — admin (service_role, for API routes)
- Remove: `client.ts`, simplify `middleware.ts` to reuse `server.ts`
**Effort:** 30min
**Benefit:** Less confusion, single source of truth

---

## P1 — MEDIUM IMPACT REFACTORS

### 4. localStorage Key Constants
**Files:** 6 files with 10+ localStorage key strings
**Issue:** `viajero_currency`, `viajero_lang`, `viajero_favs`, `viajero_favs_${id}`, `viajero_cart_activity`, `viajero_cart_reminder_sent`, `viajero_promos`
**Proposal:** `lib/storage-keys.ts`
```typescript
export const STORAGE_KEYS = {
  CURRENCY: "viajero_currency",
  LANG: "viajero_lang",
  FAVORITES: (userId?: string) => userId ? `viajero_favs_${userId}` : "viajero_favs",
  CART_ACTIVITY: "viajero_cart_activity",
  CART_REMINDER: "viajero_cart_reminder_sent",
  PROMOS: "viajero_promos",
} as const
```
**Effort:** 15min

### 5. Abandoned Cart / Cart Recovery Module
**Files:** `lib/abandoned-cart.ts`
**Issue:** Scattered across localStorage reads/writes, could be a clean service
**Also:** Related config lives in `content/es.json` (abandonedCart section)
**Proposal:** Consolidate into single service with config injection

### 6. Auth Context De-dup
**File:** `lib/auth-context.tsx` (200+ lines)
**Issue:** Giant context with login, register, OAuth, profile, addresses, favorites, orders ALL in one file
**Proposal:** Split into:
- `lib/auth/auth-context.tsx` — core auth (login, register, logout, session)
- `lib/auth/profile-hooks.ts` — profile, addresses
- `lib/auth/orders-hooks.ts` — orders
- `lib/auth/favorites.ts` — favorites (localStorage)

---

## P2 — LOW IMPACT / CODE QUALITY

### 7. Header Component Abstract Sections
**File:** `components/header.tsx` (357 lines)
**Issue:** Largest component. Mixes nav, search, currency, lang, cart, auth, dark mode
**Proposal:** Extract into smaller sub-components:
- `components/header/nav.tsx` — navigation links
- `components/header/actions.tsx` — search, currency, lang, dark mode
- `components/header/cart-badge.tsx` — cart icon + count
- `components/header/auth-menu.tsx` — login / user menu

### 8. Admin Layout Split
**File:** `components/admin/admin-layout.tsx` (143 lines)
**Issue:** Contains auth check, sidebar, header, content area — could split into sidebar + header
**Proposal:**
- `components/admin/sidebar.tsx`
- `components/admin/admin-header.tsx`

### 9. Cart Context Simplify
**File:** `lib/cart-context.tsx` (117 lines)
**Issue:** Interface CartItem, Context, Provider, add/remove/update/clear all in one file
**Proposal:** Extract CartItem type to separate file, keep context focused

### 10. Magic Numbers
**File:** `lib/currency.tsx`
**Issue:** `RATE_PYG_PER_USD = 7400` hardcoded — should come from config or API
**Proposal:** Move to `content/es.json` (paymentGateway section has rate field already) or fetch from external API

---

## ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM (current vs proposed)

### Current:
```
app/api/
├── checkout/
│   ├── bancard/route.ts    ← 4 nearly identical files
│   ├── pagopar/route.ts
│   ├── paypal/route.ts
│   └── stripe/route.ts
├── db/
│   ├── orders/route.ts     ← 5 nearly identical CRUD files
│   ├── products/route.ts
│   ├── promos/route.ts
│   ├── reviews/route.ts
│   └── users/route.ts
├── auth/route.ts
├── orders/route.ts
└── ...

lib/
├── cart-context.tsx         ← 117 lines, 5 concerns
├── auth-context.tsx         ← 200+ lines, 6 concerns
├── supabase/
│   ├── client.ts             ← redundant (re-exports browser.ts)
│   ├── browser.ts
│   ├── server.ts
│   ├── middleware.ts
│   └── admin.ts
│   
components/
├── header.tsx                ← 357 lines, 6 sub-components
├── admin/admin-layout.tsx    ← 143 lines, 3 sections
```

### Proposed:
```
app/api/
├── checkout/[gateway]/route.ts  ← 1 file, delegates to adapters
├── db/
│   ├── [[resource]]/route.ts   ← 1 file, factory-generated
│   └── ...

lib/
├── supabase/
│   ├── browser.ts
│   ├── server.ts
│   └── admin.ts                ← client.ts + middleware.ts removed
├── payment/
│   ├── factory.ts              ← handles routing + validation
│   ├── bancard.ts              ← gateway adapter (20 lines)
│   ├── pagopar.ts
│   ├── paypal.ts
│   └── stripe.ts
├── db/
│   └── crud-factory.ts         ← generates GET/POST from table name
├── auth/
│   ├── auth-context.tsx
│   ├── profile-hooks.ts
│   ├── orders-hooks.ts  
│   └── favorites.ts
├── storage-keys.ts             ← single source for all localStorage keys
├── cart-context.tsx             ← same size but cleaner

components/
├── header/
│   ├── index.tsx
│   ├── nav.tsx
│   ├── actions.tsx
│   ├── cart-badge.tsx
│   └── auth-menu.tsx
├── admin/
│   ├── admin-layout.tsx
│   ├── sidebar.tsx
│   └── admin-header.tsx
```

## QUICK WINS (15 min each)
1. Create `lib/storage-keys.ts` — eliminates magic string risk
2. Remove `lib/supabase/client.ts` — it's a redundant re-export
3. Consolidate `lib/currency.tsx` rate into content config
4. Extract `lib/cart-context.tsx` CartItem type to `lib/types.ts`

## MEDIUM WINS (1-2h each)
5. Payment gateway factory (2h) — biggest RoI
6. DB CRUD factory (1h) — cleanest abstraction
7. Auth context split (1h) — maintainability

## BIG WINS (3-4h each)
8. Payment gateway factory + test suite
9. Full architecture reorg (header split, admin split, supabase consolidation)
10. Shared package extraction to @elviajero/common (if any other site would benefit)
"""

write_file("/root/elviajero-comercio/docs/refactoring-analysis.md", analysis)
print("Refactoring analysis saved to /root/elviajero-comercio/docs/refactoring-analysis.md")
"}

**Hallazgos principales:**

| Patrón | Archivos | Líneas | Propuesta |
|--------|----------|--------|-----------|
| 🏭 **Payment Gateways** | 4 routes (bancard, pagopar, paypal, stripe) | 33-43 c/u | Factory pattern → 1 ruta parametrizada |

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import os

# Fix all corrupted admin files
path = "/root/elviajero-comercio"
admin_files = [
    "app/admin/page.tsx", "app/admin/pedidos/page.tsx", "app/admin/productos/page.tsx",
    "app/admin/categorias/page.tsx", "app/admin/importar/page.tsx", "app/admin/promos/page.tsx",
    "app/admin/reportes/page.tsx", "app/admin/resenas/page.tsx", "app/admin/suscriptores/page.tsx",
    "app/admin/tema/page.tsx", "app/admin/usuarios/page.tsx",
]

for f in admin_files:
    fp = f"{path}/{f}"
    if os.path.exists(fp):
        # Fix the line number corruption (pattern: "N|content" -> "content")
        r = terminal(f"sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*[0-9]\\+|//' '{fp}'", timeout=3)
        # Check if the first line still has corruption
        with open(fp) as fh:
            first = fh.readline()
        if first.startswith("    1|") or first.startswith("1|"):
            with open(fp) as fh:
                content = fh.read()
            # Remove all "N|" or "     N|" prefixes
            import re
            content = re.sub(r'^[ \t]*\d+\|', '', content, flags=re.MULTILINE)
            with open(fp, 'w') as fh:
                fh.write(content)

print("Admin files fixed")
"}

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import os, re

path = "/root/elviajero-comercio"

# Find all corrupted files (those with "N|" at line start)
r = terminal(f"grep -rln '^[[:space:]]*[0-9]\\+[[:space:]]*|' --include='*.tsx' --include='*.ts' {path}/app/ {path}/components/ {path}/lib/ 2>/dev/null | head -30", timeout=8)
print(f"Corrupted files: {r['output']}")

for fp in r["output"].strip().split("\n"):
    fp = fp.strip()
    if not fp:
        continue
    with open(fp) as f:
        content = f.read()
    # Remove ALL "    N|content" or "N|content" patterns
    cleaned = re.sub(r'^[ \t]*\d+\|', '', content, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    if cleaned != content:
        with open(fp, 'w') as f:
            f.write(cleaned)
        print(f"  Fixed: {fp}")

print("All corruption fixed")
"}

{"content": "     1|"use client"
     2|import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "react"
     3|import { createClient } from "@/lib/supabase/client"
     4|import type { User as SupabaseUser } from "@supabase/supabase-js"
     5|
     6|export interface User {
     7|  id: string
     8|  name: string
     9|  email: string
    10|  phone: string
    11|  role: string
    12|  createdAt: string
    13|}
    14|
    15|export interface Address {
    16|  id: string
    17|  label: string
    18|  name: string
    19|  street: string
    20|  city: string
    21|  state: string
    22|  zip: string
    23|  phone: string
    24|  isDefault: boolean
    25|}
    26|
    27|export interface OrderItem {
    28|  name: string
    29|  price: string
    30|  quantity: number
    31|  imageUrl?: string
    32|}
    33|
    34|export interface Order {
    35|  id: string
    36|  date: string
    37|  items: OrderItem[]
    38|  total: string
    39|  status: "pendiente" | "confirmado" | "enviado" | "entregado" | "cancelado"
    40|  addressId: string
    41|  paymentMethod: string
    42|}
    43|
    44|interface AuthContextType {
    45|  user: User | null
    46|  loading: boolean
    47|  login: (email: string, password: [REDACTED] => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    48|  register: (name: string, email: string, password: [REDACTED] phone: string) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    49|  loginWithGoogle: () => Promise<void>
    50|  loginWithFacebook: () => Promise<void>
    51|  logout: () => Promise<void>
    52|  updateProfile: (data: Partial<User>) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    53|  changePassword: [REDACTED] string, newPass: string) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    54|  addresses: Address[]
    55|  addAddress: (a: Omit<Address, "id">) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    56|  updateAddress: (id: string, a: Partial<Address>) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    57|  removeAddress: (id: string) => Promise<void>
    58|  orders: Order[]
    59|  refreshOrders: () => Promise<void>
    60|  addOrder: (o: Omit<Order, "id" | "date" | "status">) => Promise<string>
    61|  favorites: string[]
    62|  toggleFavorite: (productName: string) => void
    63|  isFavorite: (productName: string) => boolean
    64|}
    65|
    66|const AuthContext = createContext<AuthContextType | null>(null)
    67|
    68|export function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
    69|  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null)
    70|  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    71|  const [supabase] = useState(() => createClient())
    72|  const [addresses, setAddresses] = useState<Address[]>([])
    73|  const [orders, setOrders] = useState<Order[]>([])
    74|  const [favorites, setFavorites] = useState<string[]>([])
    75|
    76|  const refreshOrders = useCallback(async () => {
    77|    if (!user) return
    78|    const { data } = await supabase
    79|      .from("ej_orders")
    80|      .select("*")
    81|      .eq("user_id", user.id)
    82|      .order("created_at", { ascending: false })
    83|    if (data) setOrders(data.map((o: any) => ({ ...o, id: o.id || "", date: o.created_at || o.date, items: o.items || [], total: o.total || "0", status: o.status || "pendiente", addressId: o.address_id || "", paymentMethod: o.payment_method || "" })))
    84|  }, [user, supabase])
    85|
    86|  const loadAddresses = useCallback(async () => {
    87|    if (!user) return
    88|    const { data } = await supabase
    89|      .from("addresses")
    90|      .select("*")
    91|      .eq("user_id", user.id)
    92|      .order("is_default", { ascending: false })
    93|    if (data) setAddresses(data.map((a: any) => ({ id: a.id, label: a.label || "", name: a.name || "", street: a.street, city: a.city, state: a.state || "", zip: a.zip || "", phone: a.phone || "", isDefault: a.is_default })))
    94|  }, [user, supabase])
    95|
    96|  const fetchProfile = useCallback(async (supabaseUser: SupabaseUser) => {
    97|    try {
    98|      const { data } = await supabase
    99|        .from("profiles")
   100|        .select("*")
   101|        .eq("id", supabaseUser.id)
   102|        .single()
   103|      if (data) {
   104|        setUser({ id: data.id, name: data.name || supabaseUser.email?.split("@")[0] || "", email: supabaseUser.email || "", phone: data.phone || "", role: data.role || "customer", createdAt: data.created_at || "" })
   105|      } else {
   106|        setUser({ id: supabaseUser.id, name: supabaseUser.email?.split("@")[0] || "", email: supabaseUser.email || "", phone: "", role: "customer", createdAt: supabaseUser.created_at || "" })
   107|      }
   108|    } catch {
   109|      setUser({ id: supabaseUser.id, name: supabaseUser.email?.split("@")[0] || "", email: supabaseUser.email || "", phone: "", role: "customer", createdAt: supabaseUser.created_at || "" })
   110|    }
   111|  }, [supabase])
   112|
   113|  useEffect(() => {
   114|    supabase.auth.getSession().then(({ data: { session } }) => {
   115|      if (session?.user) fetchProfile(session.user)
   116|      setLoading(false)
   117|    })
   118|
   119|    const { data: { subscription } } = supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((_event, session) => {
   120|      if (session?.user) { fetchProfile(session.user); loadAddresses(); refreshOrders() }
   121|      else { setUser(null); setAddresses([]); setOrders([]) }
   122|    })
   123|
   124|    return () => subscription.unsubscribe()
   125|  }, [supabase, fetchProfile, loadAddresses, refreshOrders])
   126|
   127|  const login = useCallback(async (email: string, password: [REDACTED] => {
   128|    const { error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })
   129|    if (error) return { ok: false, error: error.message === "Invalid login credentials" ? "Credenciales incorrectas" : error.message }
   130|    return { ok: true }
   131|  }, [supabase])
   132|
   133|  const register = useCallback(async (name: string, email: string, password: [REDACTED] phone: string) => {
   134|    const { error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
   135|      email,
   136|      password,
   137|      options: { data: { name, phone } },
   138|    })
   139|    if (error) {
   140|      if (error.message.includes("already registered")) return { ok: false, error: "Email ya registrado" }
   141|      return { ok: false, error: error.message }
   142|    }
   143|    return { ok: true }
   144|  }, [supabase])
   145|
   146|  const loginWithGoogle = useCallback(async () => {
   147|    await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({
   148|      provider: "google",
   149|      options: { redirectTo: `${window.location.origin}/auth/callback` },
   150|    })
   151|  }, [supabase])
   152|
   153|  const loginWithFacebook = useCallback(async () => {
   154|    await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({
   155|      provider: "facebook",
   156|      options: { redirectTo: `${window.location.origin}/auth/callback` },
   157|    })
   158|  }, [supabase])
   159|
   160|  const logout = useCallback(async () => {
   161|    await supabase.auth.signOut()
   162|    setUser(null)
   163|    setAddresses([])
   164|    setOrders([])
   165|    setFavorites([])
   166|  }, [supabase])
   167|
   168|  const updateProfile = useCallback(async (data: Partial<User>) => {
   169|    if (!user) return { ok: false, error: "No hay sesión" }
   170|    const updates: any = {}
   171|    if (data.name) updates.name = data.name
   172|    if (data.phone !== undefined) updates.phone = data.phone
   173|    const { error } = await supabase.from("profiles").update(updates).eq("id", user.id)
   174|    if (error) return { ok: false, error: error.message }
   175|    setUser({ ...user, ...data })
   176|    return { ok: true }
   177|  }, [user, supabase])
   178|

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter (all). Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) - shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content still lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) now comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- SQLite database for orders and admin
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "import os, re

path = "/root/elviajero-comercio"

# Comprehensive fix for ALL corrupted files
for root, dirs, files in os.walk(os.path.join(path, "app")):
    for f in files:
        if f.endswith(('.tsx', '.ts')):
            fp = os.path.join(root, f)
            with open(fp) as fh:
                content = fh.read()
            # Remove ALL "N|content" line-number prefixes (any indentation)
            cleaned = re.sub(r'^[ \t]*\d+\|', '', content, flags=re.MULTILINE)
            if cleaned != content:
                with open(fp, 'w') as fh:
                    fh.write(cleaned)
                print(f"Fixed: {fp[len(path):]}")

print("All corruption fixed")
"}

The ANON_KEY uses `${SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}` as a Docker Compose variable interpolation, not as a shell env var. These variables need to be set in the shell environment or in a `.env` file. But the real issue: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` IS hardcoded, but `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` uses variable interpolation. Since the env var is not set in the shell, it ends up empty.

Let me hardcode the ANON_KEY from the content file into docker-compose.yml.

# Multi-Repo Architecture Analysis

Analyze N repos for duplication, shared patterns, and extractable packages. 
Produces a phased refactoring roadmap, then EXECUTES the improvements across ALL repos.

User preference: Ivan prefers parallel batch operations, zero multi-choice questions.
When the analysis is complete, DO NOT STOP to ask "which phase next?" — execute all
phases immediately. This skill's job is ANALYZE → PLAN → BUILD → APPLY → VERIFY.

## Workflow

### 1. Collect Repo Inventory

Gather structure, deps, and config from every repo:

```bash
for repo in /tmp/paragu-ai-builder /tmp/dayah-litworks /tmp/nexa-paraguay /tmp/elviajero-comercio; do
  if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then
    echo "=== NAME ===" && basename "$repo"
    echo "--- Top-level files ---" && ls "$repo" | head -15
    echo "--- Dockerfile ---" && wc -l "$repo/Dockerfile" 2>/dev/null
    echo "--- package.json deps ---" && node -e "const p=require('$repo/package.json'); Object.entries({...p.dependencies,...p.devDependencies}).forEach(([k,v])=>console.log(k,v))" 2>/dev/null | head -20
  fi
done
```

### 2. Identify Duplication Vectors

Check these across ALL repos:

| Vector | What to Check |
|--------|---------------|
| **Dockerfile** | Stage structure, base image, build commands — identical? |
| **PostCSS / Tailwind config** | Same plugins, same version? |
| **Middleware** | i18n detection, locale routing — any shared logic? |
| **Layout (layout.tsx)** | Header/Footer wrappers, font loading — duplicated? |
| **Content schema** | `content/{locale}.json` + `content/tokens.json` — same structure? |
| **UI components** | Button, Card, Footer, WhatsAppFloat — each repo reinvents? |
| **Not-found / error pages** | Same patterns, different implementations? |
| **Analytics** | GA4, Meta pixel, Hotjar loaders — one version or many? |

### 3. Map the Architecture — Classification First

Before analyzing, classify each repo into one of 5 architecture patterns:

| Pattern | Description | Examples | Components | Content |
|---------|-------------|----------|-----------|---------|
| **A: Full E-commerce** | Next.js + SQLite/Supabase + Auth + Admin + Multi-payment | elviajero-comercio | 107 | ES/EN/GN, 70KB |
| **B: Static Showcase** | Next.js + content-driven + client-kit components | dayah, superspuma, depiflash, ozmontania, 3md, bichos-gym, cocodrilo, mantra, luis-de-leon, magnolia | 6-7 shared | ES, tokens.json |
| **C: Scaffold-only** | Next.js scaffold, minimal real pages | golden-visa, stroopwafel, granja-cabral | 0 | N/A or skeleton |
| **D: Complex App** | Next.js + Supabase/PostgreSQL + custom features | clinica-duerksen, fun4me | 28+ | DB-backed or JSON |
| **E: PAB Tenant** | Inside paragu-ai-builder monorepo, not standalone | alejandro-villamayor (still in PAB) | Engine sections | sites/{tenant}/ |

**Key insight for AI agents:** If you see a repo with ~7 components that includes header, footer, whatsapp-float, cookie-consent, cta-banner, mobile-cta, process-section — it's a Type B Static Showcase forked from the same template. All Type B repos have near-identical component code. Use the client-kit repo or template-nextjs-client as the canonical source.

Document the current topology:

```
paragu-ai.com/                          ← Type A: Monolith (PAB)
  s/{locale}/{tenant}/...               ← Dynamic tenant routes via engine

tenant1.paragu-ai.com                   ← Type B: Standalone Docker service
tenant2.paragu-ai.com                   ← Each has own Dockerfile, build, port, Traefik
```

### 4. Quantify Overlap Before Recommending

For each duplication vector, estimate:
- **LoC duplicated** — how many lines of near-identical code
- **Maintenance cost** — how many places to fix a bug / update a dep
- **Extraction effort** — how hard to pull into a shared package

### 5. Produce Refactoring Roadmap

Phased structure:

**Phase 1: Low-hanging fruit** — config files, Dockerfile templates, postcss.
  > Extract `Dockerfile.template`, publish base image, create shared postcss config.

**Phase 2: Value package** — UI components used by every site.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/ui` (Button, Card, Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat, CookieConsent, Badge, Breadcrumb).

**Phase 3: Domain packages** — analytics, tokens/schema, content resolution.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/tokens` (color palette schema + CSS var resolution), `@paragu-ai/analytics`.

**Phase 4: Commerce/engine** — complex shared logic.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/commerce` (cart interface, checkout flow), `@paragu-ai/engine` (section composition).

### 6. Build Shared Packages

After identifying shared patterns, build packages in THIS order:

1. **Docker base image** — build + publish FIRST so every app Dockerfile can reference it immediately
2. **Token package** — schema + CSS var resolution (smallest, lowest risk, highest reusability)
3. **WhatsApp/utility package** — URL builders + float component (2–3 files, used by every site)
4. **UI package** — Button, Card, Toast, ErrorBoundary, Skeleton, Badge, Breadcrumb, Dialog
5. **Analytics/i18n/SEO packages** — parallel, they have no interdependencies
6. **Engine/Commerce** — longest lead time, dependent on types from UI package

For each package:
- Write `.ts` sources in `packages/<name>/src/`
- Each gets its own `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
- Root `pnpm-workspace.yaml` includes `packages/*`
- `tsconfig.base.json` at root with `target: ES2022, module: ESNext, jsx: react-jsx`
- `.npmrc` at root for GitHub Packages auth (scope matches org name: `@ai-whisperers`)

Build with:
```bash
TSC="/home/paragu-ai/node_modules/.bin/tsc"
for pkg in tokens ui whatsapp analytics i18n seo engine commerce; do
  cd /home/paragu-ai/packages/$pkg
  $TSC --noEmit && $TSC
done
```

Publish to GitHub Packages:
```bash
# Must use org scope (e.g. @ai-whisperers, NOT @paragu-ai)
# .npmrc must have inline token (not env var expansion)
npm publish --userconfig /home/paragu-ai/.npmrc
```

Publish Docker base image to GHCR:
```bash
docker build -f Dockerfile.base -t paragu-ai/next-base:node20 .
docker tag ... ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
```

### 7. Apply Improvements Across ALL Repos

For each improvement type, iterate ALL repos:

**Dockerfiles:**
```bash
# Standard template:
FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps
... (COPY, npm install, build, runner stages)
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
# ADD extra COPY lines per-repo if they have ./data, ./db, etc.
```

**Traefik labels:**
```bash
# Add via docker service update (won't persist to TaskTemplate)
# Better: deploy via docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml
# Even better: centralize all routes in /opt/traefik/dynamic/apps.yml
service update --label-add "traefik.enable=true" ...
```

**.npmrc:**
```bash
echo "@ai-whisperers:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/" >> .npmrc
echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=$(gh auth token)" >> .npmrc
```

**Commit per repo:**
```bash
git add Dockerfile .npmrc package.json
git commit -m "refactor: standardize Dockerfile, add .npmrc"
git push
```

Watch for:
- **Empty repos** (alejandro-villamayor had zero files) — need full scaffold
- **Archived repos** (nexa-paraguay was archived as nexa-paraguay-docs) — unarchive first
- **Node_modules in git** (fun4me had 136MB tracked) — need `--orphan` clean branch + force push
- **Branch protection rules** preventing push — use `HEAD:main` or ask user
- **Cross-org dupes** (IvanWeissVanDerPol/Vete vs Ai-Whisperers/Vete) — delete from personal org
- **Repos with no app/ directory** — nico-portfolio, granja-cabral are data-only repos, not Next.js apps. Don't try to run `find app/` on them. Check `[ -d app/ ]` before page discovery.
- **Content varies between repos** — Type B repos may have `content/es.json` (static-showcase sites) or `content/site.json` (auto-generated minimal sites). Type D repos may have NO content/ dir at all (DB-backed). Always check with `ls content/ 2>/dev/null` before assuming schema.

### 8. Verify

```bash
# Check all sites respond 200
for domain in paragu-ai.com dayah.paragu-ai.com el-viajero.paragu-ai.com; do
  curl -so /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://$domain
done

# Check all Docker services have the expected replicas
docker service ls --format "{{.Name}} {{.Replicas}}"

# Verify no nginx zombie processes
pgrep nginx | wc -l  # should be 0
```

## Variant: Client Extraction from Paragu-ai Builder Monorepo

This pattern handles extracting client site data from `paragu-ai-builder/sites/` + `web/app/{client}/` into standalone repos, mirroring the pattern used for dayah-litworks, elviajero-comercio, cocodrilo-fitness, etc.

### When to Use

The monorepo at `Ai-Whisperers/paragu-ai-builder` is the ENGINE/TEMPLATE — it contains:
- `web/` — Next.js app with 108 section components, 47 UI components
- `src/` — 1000+ business type definitions (registry, tokens, schemas, content, verticals)
- `supabase/` — database schema
- `sites/` — **client data** to be extracted (content, images, configs)

Standalone repos are FULL Next.js apps per client. The monorepo keeps the reusable engine; each client gets its own deployable repo.

### Discovery Workflow

1. **Inventory what's in the builder:**
```bash
# "Real" client sites (not demos/previews)
ls sites/ | grep -v '^demo-' | grep -v '^preview-' | grep -v '^_' | \
  grep -v 'client-kit' | grep -v 'image-prompts' | grep -v 'lead-flyers' | \
  grep -v 'shared-images' | grep -v 'outreach-' | grep -v '^premium' | sort
```

2. **Check what's already standalone:**
```bash
# Any repo dir in /root/ that has a git remote
for d in /root/*/; do
  if [ -d "$d/.git" ]; then
    url=$(cd "$d" 2>/dev/null && git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    [ -n "$url" ] && echo "$(basename $d) -> $url"
  fi
done
```

3. **Check for hardcoded app routes** in the builder:
```bash
ls web/app/ | grep -v '^\[business\]' | grep -v '^admin' | grep -v '^api' | \
  grep -v '^auth' | grep -v '^blog' | grep -v '^demo' | grep -v '^en' | \
  grep -v '^s\|^p\|^c'
```

4. **Check what's in each client's data directory:**
```bash
for s in client1 client2; do
  echo "=== $s ===" && ls sites/$s/
done
```

### Current Extraction Targets (as of [REDACTED_PHONE])

| Client | Sites Dir | App Routes | Already Standalone? | Notes |
|--------|-----------|------------|-------------------|-------|
| dayah-litworks | sites/dayah-litworks | web/app/dayah-litworks | ✅ | Already extracted, 10 pages, 7 components |
| fun4me | sites/fun4me | ❌ | ✅ | Already extracted, 30+ pages, 28 commits |

| nexa-paraguay | sites/nexa-paraguay | web/app/nexa-paraguay | ✅ | Extracted, has hardcoded app route |
| nexa-propiedades | sites/nexa-propiedades | ❌ | ✅ | Extracted from builder |
